Label: Logical Reasoning
Description: Objectively analyse, evaluate, and interpret information and arguments in a logical and systematic manner in order to arrive at conclusions.
Area Description: Possess the ability to analyse facts, reports, policies, precedents, constraints, risks, and conflicting points of view in a comprehensive and logical manner to gain a holistic perspective and arrive at a timely decision. It involves weighing the pros and cons of various options to arrive at the best possible decision that would lead to the welfare of citizens and contribute towards attainment of Aatma Nirbhar & Viksit Bharat.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Identifies and categorises relevant and credible information sources, collecting new data from internal and external sources (such as World Bank publications and Indian government reports) to inform decision-making at the ministries, departments, and organisations.   Differentiates between primary and secondary sources, cross-validating collected data against multiple credible sources to ensure accuracy and reliability in the decision-making process.  Understands structured data collection techniques (such as surveys, questionnaires, and interviews) and systematically applies these methods to gather information using basic research principles.  Organises data logically through categorisation, labelling, and structuring, ensuring clarity and ease of retrieval for future analysis.  Documents findings in a systematic manner using standardised formats, facilitating future analysis and reporting while ensuring consistency and accessibility of information.   Understands various logical and critical thinking frameworks (such as SWOT analysis and decision trees) and applies these tools to enhance analytical thinking and decision-making processes.
Levels.Output_Description: I collect and categorise credible information from diverse internal and external sources.  I verify the accuracy of information by cross-referencing multiple credible sources.  I organise and document data in a structured and logical manner for easy retrieval and future analysis.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Evaluates complex data and information to identify significant trends, risks, and patterns that inform decision-making, demonstrating analytical thinking and attention to detail.  Utilises structured tools such as SWOT analysis, decision matrices, and risk assessments to systematically evaluate options, fostering a collaborative environment that encourages team input and diverse perspectives.  Utilises behavioural frameworks to prioritise solutions based on their potential impact and feasibility.  Demonstrates flexibility by adapting frameworks to meet the specific context and requirements of the decision-making process at the ministries, departments, and organisations.   Facilitates scenario analysis to explore multiple potential outcomes, assessing the risks and benefits associated with each decision, thereby promoting critical thinking and sound judgement among team members.  Guides the team towards consensus on decisions through logical reasoning and collaborative problem-solving, enhancing communication skills and ensuring that all voices are heard.  Performs basic risk analysis while organising and validating data to ensure the credibility and accuracy of the information collected, exemplifying diligence and a commitment to high-quality standards in the decision-making process.
Levels.Output_Description: I integrate insights from multiple complex data sources to inform tactical decision-making. I apply structured tools such as SWOT analysis, decision matrices, and risk assessment matrices to prioritise options and guide strategic choices. I reach consensus on decisions that reflect the collective input of cross-functional teams.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Demonstrates strategic foresight by actively engaging in forecasting techniques and scenario planning  to assess the long-term implications of decisions on organisational and national objectives.  Exhibits a commitment to integrating insights from diverse data sources, such as economic forecasts, social trends, and environmental factors to create comprehensive analyses that inform decision-making at the ministries, departments, and organisations.    Actively engages in transparent dialogue to evaluate the potential impact of strategic decisions on stakeholders, ensuring alignment with ethical standards and organisational values.  Ensures strategic alignment of decisions with national initiatives such as Aatma Nirbhar Bharat, instilling a sense of national commitment, and encouraging the team to prioritise sustainable development and citizen welfare.  Engages with key stakeholders to collaboratively articulate a shared vision that reflects national priorities, fostering open communication and building consensus.  Exhibits a commitment to continuous improvement by regularly monitoring and assessing the effectiveness of strategies in achieving desired outcomes, making necessary adjustments to stay aligned with evolving national goals.  Adopts cutting-edge technologies (such as machine learning algorithms, natural language processing, blockchain, etc.) to enhance logical reasoning and innovates  the accuracy of decision-making processes.   Employs advanced risk management techniques, such as predictive modelling, to enhance the robustness of decision making.
Levels.Output_Description: I align strategic decisions with national priorities using technology to improve decision making across M/D/Os.  I employ advanced risk management techniques to identify and mitigate potential risks, enhancing the robustness of decision-making and ensuring alignment with evolving organisational and national goals. I promote transparency and accountability by clearly communicating strategic decisions and facilitating collaborative input from diverse teams.

Label: Analytical Thinking
Description: Demonstrates ability to extract key information from data, reports, precedence through thorough examination, scrutiny, and analysis using logic to address issues and problems.
Area Description: Displays a sense of carefulness and practicality in employing both creative and reflective problem-solving methods. Conducts thorough root-cause analyses and collaborates with experts and stakeholders to generate a range of hypotheses based on historical precedents, data, reports, with a goal to anticipate potential issues and suggest practical and alternative solutions that are based on insights gained from a comprehensive analysis of all relevant available information.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Maintains attention to detail to obtain, and effectively sift through comprehensive information including reports, briefs, policy papers, data, etc.  Understands policies and standards applicable to the M/D/O and adheres to them in decision making for proposals, schemes, and guidelines.  Collects, analyses and breaks down complex issues into smaller parts for easier analysis from a variety of sources (such as statistical serials, census reports, budget  papers, economic surveys, etc).   Checks the source of data, ensures relevance and validity, and formats data accordingly.  Understands the links between situations and given information to draw meaningful information, and address issues and problems.  Plans own work thoroughly and meticulously by using planning tools such as work plans, checklists, etc and supports the introduction of better ways of working (for e.g., using softwares such as Trello or Miro to manage work plans).
Levels.Output_Description: I pay close attention to detail while extracting and reviewing information, ensuring accuracy and relevance.  I collect and analyse complex issues from various sources, connecting information to draw meaningful insights and address problems.  I plan and organise my own work, using planning tools and introducing efficient methods to improve workflows.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Identifies critical patterns in information and data, to recognise root causes of actions that are not readily apparent for delivery of high standard citizen-centric outcomes (for e.g., identifying cause and effect relationships, etc).  Interprets information to identify discrepancies and inconsistencies to optimize workflows, ensure standards and guidelines are adhered to, tasks are completed, documents are reviewed, and re-work is avoided/minimized. Transforms self, holds others accountable and monitors quality of work for delivery of high standard outcomes, adherence to policies and procedures, and provision of support/guidance where necessary by paying more attention to detail.  Prepares detailed records of discussions and agreed actions to ensure that information is accurate and prompt follow-up occurs where applicable to develop potential solutions, and identify risks.   Reviews problems and digs deeper by asking questions, examining literature or consulting experts (such as policymakers, academics, civil society members, etc) when not satisfied with the level of detail.  Assesses multiple cause-and-effect relationships in a problem, with the ability to see several potential causes of an event.  Supports in designing solutions that attempt to address the complexities at different levels in a practical manner to assess impact in the short, medium, and long term.   Monitors compliance to procedures and regulatory requirements and takes action in case of any deviation with appropriate procedures (such as taking corrective and preventative actions, root cause analysis, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FEMA), etc).
Levels.Output_Description: I identify and interpret patterns and gaps in information, using root cause analysis to improve processes and public service delivery.   I assess information and  design practical solutions collaboratively to address potential challenges and risks.   I comply with policies and apply corrective measures when necessary.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Resolves broader issues about risk and compliance, quality of work, and service delivery to avoid unnecessary risks.  Designs solutions that attempt to address the complexities at different levels in a practical manner to assess impact in the short, medium, and long term.   Oversees  systemic spot checks and balances (for e.g., by doing impact assessments, using a matrix analysis, force field analysis, affinity diagrams, etc).   Assesses the level of pressure or influence to apply with respect to each analysis conducted, with relation to the broader context and meeting specific M/D/O goals.  Leads strategy and approach based on a whole systems’ perspective after synthesizing relevant information.   Builds consensus among diverse parties/stakeholders by assessing common interests, and taking a long-term view of public good.
Levels.Output_Description: I resolve issues around risk, compliance, and service delivery using sound judgement.   I design practical solutions by considering complexities and assessing short-term and long-term impacts.   I create strategies using a systems-thinking approach, ensuring thorough assessments and balancing stakeholders interests.

Label: Sound Judgment
Description: Ability to make well-informed, rational, and balanced decisions based on a thorough analysis of available information, careful consideration of potential consequences, and alignment with ethical principles and  M/D/O goals
Area Description: Possess the ability to analyse facts, reports, policies, precedents, constraints, risks, and conflicting points of view in a comprehensive and logical manner to gain a holistic perspective and arrive at a timely decision. It involves weighing the pros and cons of various options to arrive at the best possible decision that would lead to the welfare of citizens and contribute towards the attainment of Aatma Nirbhar & Viksit Bharat.

Label: Relationship Management
Description: Actively establishes connections, nurtures relationships, and capitalizes on opportunities to identify intelligence and insights into existing or potential challenges. Additionally, builds partnerships and public-private collaborations to leverage expertise in the national and global ecosystem, enhancing efficiency and promoting social inclusion for pro-people governance.
Area Description: Working in tandem with others as an integral unit, establishing partnerships and cooperative working relationships toward realizing Division / Department / Ministry goals. Proactively sharing relevant and useful knowledge, experience, or expertise with peers/team to strengthen knowledge base. It also refers to leveraging diverse strengths and contribution of every officer/staff for timely and high-quality delivery.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Participates honestly and fairly while dealing with others, showing consideration and other tangential skills (for e.g., respect, politeness, emotional intelligence, active listening, etc).  Engages with colleagues, stakeholders, beneficiaries with empathy and professionalism fostering trust in day to day interactions.  Practices a positive attitude towards team members (for e.g., by giving feedback, sharing knowledge, approaching problems with empathy, etc).   Engages with local communities and beneficiaries to understand their needs, improving service delivery and supporting pro-people governance. Adopts digital tools (e.g., WhatsApp groups for coordination, mobile apps for reporting issues) to enhance engagement, reducing administrative burden, and enhancing efficiency. Conveys the work of others, and speaks positively about team members to build team spirit (for e.g., at team meetings, M/D/O wide recognition, using 360 degree feedback, etc).  Collaborates with others to meet their own and common goals and to address challenges and escalations if required.  Identifies and shares insights from the field by observing trends, gathering citizen feedback, and reporting relevant observations to supervisors at the tactical level. Collaborates with colleagues to align efforts, share resources, and work towards common goals efficiently.
Levels.Output_Description: I communicate clearly and professionally.  I use digital tools to improve coordination and enhance engagement.  I collaborate with others to achieve shared goals.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Builds and/or maintains partnerships through sharing of knowledge and best practice, treating confidential information with due care, in compliance with the Agency’s regulations, rules and policies.  Shares key insights and lessons learned from implementation challenges to refine strategies and improve outcomes.  Supports capacity-building initiatives by fostering knowledge exchange between agencies, departments, and external partners.  Communicates with conviction and clarity during challenging situations/complex situations.   Facilitates coordination by creating cross-functional forums and task force for internal communication, and stakeholder roundtables/meetings to foster collaboration, alignment, and shared goals.   Identifies opportunities to build relationships with external partners—such as public-private partnerships (PPPs), civil society organizations, and cross-sector collaborations—to enhance efficiency and improve public service delivery for the wider public.  Provides mentoring and guidance to peers and subordinates during implementation to ensure better collaboration and smoother execution. Conveys information clearly to internal teams and stakeholders to ensure smooth coordination and service delivery.  Expands on opportunities and efficiently utilises diverse talents of team members.  Identifies and maximizes opportunities by effectively utilizing resources (such as funds, personnel, and expertise) and  leveraging diverse team strengths for efficient service delivery.
Levels.Output_Description: I optimise resources and partnerships and external collaborations to enhance efficiency and service delivery.  I communicate field insights to share challenges and refine strategies.  I seek opportunities to build relationships with external partners and communities to serve the public.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Identifies common interests and goals, forging high-impact partnerships and joint initiatives that align with the department’s mission, vision, and long-term objectives.  Leads multi-sectoral collaborations across government, civil society, and the private sector to drive large-scale reforms and systemic change.  Evaluates and refines existing partnerships, assessing their feasibility, effectiveness, and contribution to strategic goals.  Secures strategic funding and resources, leveraging partnerships to enhance sustainability and scalability of initiatives.  Monitors and measures stakeholder impact, using data-driven insights to strengthen partnerships and improve public service delivery.  Actively builds and maintains internal and external partnerships to achieve progress on objectives and shared interests.  Inspires others to consult, keeping the greater good in mind, before arriving at any critical decisions.  Demonstrates willingness to trade-off immediate gain with long-term benefit.  Collaborates with other stakeholders such as civil society organisations, social enterprises, government agencies, to help meet the M/D/Os goals and objectives.
Levels.Output_Description: I forge high-impact partnerships and lead joint initiatives.  I enable reform at scale through sustained multi-sectoral collaboration.   I monitor stakeholder impact, using data-driven insights to refine partnerships.

Label: Influencing and Negotiation
Description: Demonstrates a high level of proficiency in shaping the perspectives of stakeholders, leading them towards acceptance of proposed approaches that benefit citizens. Engages in strategic discussions, navigates differing opinions, and achieves consensus through persuasive communication and problem-solving.
Area Description: Effectively builds alliances, facilitates negotiations, and aligns diverse stakeholder interests to drive collective decision-making. Applies diplomatic deliberation, conflict resolution, and consensus-building techniques to achieve impactful outcomes in alignment with national priorities.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Recognises engagements with diverse stakeholders, including community members, frontline workers, and officials, to address concerns, align expectations, and build trust in daily interactions. Communicates ideas effectively, adapting the message for the audience and context while integrating diverse perspectives for inclusive and impactful outreach. Uses logical reasoning, structured arguments, relatable examples, personal storytelling, and culturally relevant analogies to enhance public understanding and participation in government schemes. Facilitates citizen discussions to mediate conflicts, encourage collaboration, and build consensus on issues such as service delivery, resource allocation, and grievance redressal. Mobilises communities by leveraging peer influence, empowering early adopters as change advocates, and promoting program adoption through social proof and grassroots engagement. Negotiates with local leaders, district officials, and key partners to secure resources and services such as healthcare, education, and infrastructure for underserved communities. Upholds citizen-centric decision-making by addressing concerns, dispelling misconceptions, and overcoming resistance through consistent engagement, while also providing senior officials with well-researched insights and grassroots feedback.
Levels.Output_Description: I communicate persuasively by structuring arguments with relevant facts, policies, and relatable examples, ensuring that messages are inclusive and tailored to diverse stakeholders. I actively listen to concerns, clarify doubts, and use mediation and conflict resolution techniques to build trust, reconcile differing interests, and drive consensus in decision-making. I support senior officials by providing well-researched grassroots insights, ensuring that policies and negotiations remain citizen-centric and responsive to real-world challenges.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Conducts discussions to reconcile stakeholder interests by facilitating structured dialogue, mediating conflicts, and building consensus between government and community representatives, ensuring smooth decision-making and implementation. Anticipates resistance from citizens, bureaucratic processes, or political considerations and prepares proactive strategies such as engaging key stakeholders early, using data-driven justifications, and leveraging community influencers to address objections and drive program adoption.  Seeks feedback from citizens through surveys, public consultations, and community forums to identify concerns, refine strategies, and ensure decisions remain responsive to local needs. Engages regularly with senior officials to secure buy-in and approvals by presenting evidence-based recommendations drawn from grassroots insights Collaborates with district and block-level officers to identify implementation gaps and propose locally relevant solutions to align departmental objectives with broader governance goals.  Applies structured negotiation techniques such as interest-based bargaining and multi-stakeholder consultations to influence policies and resource allocations while ensuring diplomatic and conflict-sensitive approaches to balance competing interests, address service delivery grievances, and resolve operational bottlenecks.
Levels.Output_Description: I work closely with senior officials, district officers, and other key partners in adjusting narratives and strategies to align with both governance objectives and community needs I anticipate objections and use structured negotiation techniques, such as mediation and data-driven justifications, to address conflicts, reconcile differing interests, and drive consensus. I establish systems to streamline communication between senior officials, district & block officials and citizens to identify implementation gaps and develop locally relevant solutions.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Leads high-stakes negotiations with ministries, industries, international partners, and policymakers to secure long-term agreements on critical national initiatives, shaping policy discourse, advancing governance priorities, and driving legislative or regulatory changes. Develops influence strategies and adaptive negotiation approaches, ensuring flexible communication that reconciles competing interests and results in practical, future-ready agreements. Builds coalitions and alliances across ministries, private sector stakeholders, and global institutions to build trust, shared ownership of key reforms, and enhanced policy impact for improved service delivery. Manages high-pressure negotiations with emotional intelligence, diffusing conflicts, maintaining composure in politically sensitive discussions, and equipping teams to navigate complex negotiations effectively. Influences decision-making beyond formal authority by establishing credibility, leveraging networks, and aligning recommendations with the priorities of senior officials, legislators, and governance bodies. Balances short-term trade-offs with long-term sustainability, advocating for a forward-thinking approach that prioritises the public good while inspiring a citizen-centric governance mindset.
Levels.Output_Description: I lead high-stakes negotiations with government, industry, and global partners, using adaptive strategies and consensus-building techniques to secure agreements that drive national policy and governance priorities. I build and sustain coalitions by engaging with key stakeholders, including legislators, advisory bodies, and international organisations, building trust, shared ownership, and long-term cooperation to enhance policy impact and service delivery. I navigate complex, high-pressure environments with emotional intelligence, balancing immediate trade-offs with sustainable solutions while inspiring others to adopt a collaborative, future-focused approach.

Label: Presentation Skills
Description: Displays the ability to create documents and presentations that highlight the key points, details, accomplishments, and difficulties associated with the topic at hand.
Area Description: Exhibits the ability to speak, read and interpret official communications such as English/Hindi/ State specific in respect to their context, message, and intent. Displays positive non-verbal cues, actively practices attentive listening, endeavours to understand the viewpoints of others, and responds respectfully and humbly. Presents information with conviction, fluency, rationality, and persuasion while using communication techniques tailored to the audience.

Label: Mentoring
Description: Demonstrates the capability to foster team development by creating a supportive environment and dedicating efforts to patiently train, provide opportunities, instil discipline and commitment, and offer timely monitoring and guidance to enhance their functional efficiency and professional agility
Area Description: Fosters a positive work environment by actively engaging with the team, enabling their growth and proficiency. This involves identifying and creating opportunities to enhance officers' skills and competencies through timely feedback, guidance, and mentoring. Additionally, promoting a culture of trust, openness, appreciation, recognition, parity, and leading by example.

Label: Accountability
Description: Demonstrates the ability to take responsibility and make team accountable for measurable, high quality, timely, and cost-effective delivery. Accepts responsibility for mistakes and takes initiatives for correcting them.
Area Description: Demonstrates an intense motivation to set goals and be responsible for their achievement while striving for quality excellence. Possesses the capacity to identify problems and gaps during service delivery and to proactively analyse and assess different options to ensure best result. Drives systemic changes to increase effectiveness and efficiency in line with future needs.

Label: Process Excellence
Description: Analyse and implement government processes and procedures to ensure alignment to the department's strategic objectives and ensure value to both internal and external stakeholders.
Area Description: Establishes plans, defines milestones, and allots resources for regularly producing high-quality, cost-effective results that are in accordance with the department's or ministry's overall objectives. Focuses on tasks that will increase effectiveness, efficiency, and production through planning, intentional effort, and control. Determines goals, assigns tasks, and establishes priorities based on skill levels and the availability of resources.

Label: Attention to Detail
Description: Displays the quality of thoroughly, meticulously, and accurately examining all aspects of a problem or task, such as document creation to data analysis, budget management, and project implementation by employing a keen eye for detail for their screening and scrutiny using functional expertise and domain credibility.
Area Description: Displays a sense of carefulness and practicality in employing both creative and reflective problem-solving methods. Conducts thorough root-cause analyses and collaborates with experts and stakeholders to generate a range of hypotheses based on historical precedents, data, and reports, with a goal to anticipate potential issues and suggest practical and alternative solutions that are based on insights gained from a comprehensive analysis of all relevant available information.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Understands the policies and standards of the M/D/O and adheres to them by referencing official manuals (e.g., Central Secretariat Manual of Office Procedures, DoPT circulars, etc) to ensure that file notings, sanction proposals, and administrative approvals are in line with standard protocols and submission formats. Pays attention to detail to obtain comprehensive information by carefully reviewing documents, forms, and data entries (e.g., reconciling entries in eHRMS for staff postings), ensuring data completeness and accuracy before forwarding to supervisors for decision-making.  Understands the quality and accuracy of own and others’ work by reviewing completed case files, documents, registers, routine communications, etc in the section by using tools such as checklists, standard operating procedures, and predefined templates while flagging inconsistencies during routine reviews (e.g., by cross-checking progress trackers or pending case registers) to ensure that outputs meet required standards before final submission or circulation. Displays willingness to consult supervisors and peers to validate the details by checking procedural interpretation while processing RTI responses, drafting replies to audit observations, or clarifying applicable CCS Rules, ensuring accuracy, and avoiding missteps in sensitive communications. Recognises the significance of confidential information by following data handling protocols such as locking physical files, securing login credentials for portals like eOffice or CSCMS, and using secure communication channels when handling confidential matters like vigilance clearances, pension cases, or cadre transfers. Identifies basic discrepancies in department budgets or project reports (e.g., mismatches in GeM purchase orders or training budget utilisation reports) and highlights the issue for correction to prevent misreporting or inaccurate submissions. Follows defined processes for maintaining records and files by labelling physical folders accurately, entering data in movement registers or eOffice systems, and weeding out obsolete files under supervision, contributing to orderly and accessible document management. Provides information in a timely manner and in a usable format (e.g., forwarding complete case files, reports, or summaries to seniors and other sections), enabling others to act without delay or need for clarification. Learns from mistakes and applies lessons learned by reflecting on feedback received from supervisors, understanding the consequences of errors, and taking corrective action to prevent future oversights.
Levels.Output_Description: I examine documents, forms, or data carefully to reduce errors and ensure accuracy in my work.  I seek guidance from supervisors and peers to confirm facts or interpretations before making decisions, particularly when tasks have legal or procedural implications.  I handle sensitive information with care by using secure platforms, respecting confidentiality, and ensuring that privacy protocols are followed.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Holds self and others accountable for producing high-quality, compliant outputs by cross-verifying documents, financial records, and official drafts for factual accuracy, legal soundness, and adherence to departmental norms (e.g., CCS Rules, DoPT circulars), using structured verification tools such as checklists to reduce procedural lapses and fiscal risks. Conducts root-cause analysis of recurring errors and implements corrective actions to prevent reoccurrence and maintain accuracy in operations. Demonstrates procedural rigour by systematically tracking task progress, coordinating across teams to avoid duplication or delay, and establishing structured mechanisms (such as file registers, task sheets, etc) to enable visibility into workflows and promote accountability in routine operations in the M/D/O.  Implements internal monitoring systems by reviewing dashboards or registers to identify bottlenecks, flag delays, and verify timely submission of key records (e.g., APARs, RTI reports, training documents), initiating corrective action to ensure administrative discipline. Defines quality benchmark parameters by defining expected output standards, referencing applicable guidelines, and guiding team efforts to ensure that outputs consistently meet institutional standards.  Reviews and edits outgoing communication such as inter-ministerial letters, sanction memos, draft orders, etc, for completeness, alignment with legal and procedural standards, and internal consistency before escalating for higher-level approvals. Applies analytical thinking in managing complex cases by evaluating procedural implications (e.g., audit objections, legal notices), documenting decisions with clear rationale, maintaining audit trails, and distilling government circulars and rules into actionable guidance for consistent application in section-level workflows.
Levels.Output_Description: I drive quality assurance by applying domain-specific norms, reviewing outputs for accuracy and completeness, while incorporating  cross-functional feedback to strengthen documents and processes.   I enhance team effectiveness by validating reports, clarifying gaps, and improving internal tools to strengthen task execution.  I analyse recurring problems by using field data and expert input to guide effective solutions.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Plans and implements systemic checks and real-time validations by analysing procedural gaps, automating approvals, and using performance data and audit trails (e.g., Aadhaar-linked PDS) to reduce errors, ensure protocol compliance, and enhance efficiency, transparency, and accountability across the M/D/O. Evaluates the utility and scalability of innovative tools (e.g., blockchain for GST tracking, automated PRAGATI systems) by analysing feasibility, risks, and user feedback. Leads systemic upgrades data validation and quality control by benchmarking peer institutions to strengthen institutional workflows. Ensures precision while identifying errors or gaps in critical workflows, ensuring accuracy and reinforcing M/D/O accountability.  Champions the M/D/O’s quality management processes by aligning team practices with institutional quality standards, communicating expectations clearly, and integrating quality management protocols in routine operations. Mentors staff in advanced verification techniques through feedback-based reviews, recognises diligence in outputs, reinforces adherence to quality benchmarks, and fosters a culture of shared responsibility for procedural accuracy (such as error-free documentation) in routine operations.  Steers attention to accuracy in high-stakes legal, financial, and inter-departmental functions by validating critical inputs, identifying errors in outputs, and prioritising tasks that directly influence the strategic goals of the M/D/O.
Levels.Output_Description: I contribute to structured reviews and spot checks across teams, ensuring standards are upheld and deviations are addressed without delay.  I embed systems for quality control and use cross-departmental feedback to refine M/D/O wide service delivery.  I anticipate risks and quality issues by closely monitoring processes and regulations and propose solutions grounded in scrutiny, real-world insights, and regulatory norms.

Label: Knowledge Sharing
Description: Exchanges explicit and implicit knowledge with stakeholders at the inter-, intra-, divisional, departmental, and ministerial levels in order to improve output that leverages multiple viewpoints.
Area Description: Working in tandem with others as an integral unit, establishing partnerships and cooperative working relationships toward realizing Division / Department / Ministry goals. Proactively sharing relevant and useful knowledge, experience, or expertise with peers/team to strengthen knowledge base. It also refers to leveraging diverse strengths and contribution of every officer/staff for timely and high-quality delivery.

Label: Resilience
Description: Exhibits competence, confidence, coping skills, and self-control through awareness and mindfulness of the shared purpose of good governance and maintaining positive relations. Demonstrating professional perseverance by modifying the approach while working under increased pressure.
Area Description: Recognises one's emotional triggers and controls emotional reactions for retaining professionalism while working under pressure and heightened stress. Shows perseverance, self-control, and professional toughness when facing challenges and recovering from setbacks by adapting attitude and behaviour to meet a particular circumstance or in response to shifting working conditions and dynamics. Shows adaptability in fulfilling a given responsibility, navigating through uncertain situations with limited knowledge or resources.

Label: Self-Confidence
Description: Ability to believe in one’s own abilities, judgment, and skills and have positive and realistic self-perception and assurance to tackle challenges. Take calculated risks and persist in the face of setbacks.
Area Description: Conscious recognition of one's thoughts, emotions, and behaviours, driven by a clear sense of purpose and values. It involves a confident understanding of one's strengths and weaknesses, fostering the ability to align actions with goals and a commitment to ongoing self-learning and personal growth.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Uses feedback and self-reflection to understand strengths and areas for improvement, and then applies this awareness to complete tasks more effectively, for e.g., improving draft quality after reviewer inputs, contributing more actively in meetings, or refining a checklist before submission.  Completes standard tasks by applying templates, peer suggestions, or past experiences, showing growing confidence in routine work. For e.g., preparing file notes or selecting agenda formats using past examples. Makes routine decisions such as suggesting file routes or revising drafts without waiting for detailed guidance, demonstrating emerging judgment, and willingness to take small steps - for e.g., proposes adjustments in a checklist or drafts notes independently. Responds constructively to corrections by revising and improving work, and shows persistence in meeting expectations - for e.g., reformatting a returned draft, refining timelines after last-minute changes, or revising a concept note multiple times until accepted. Stays calm during everyday setbacks such as revisions, delays, or last-minute changes, and stays focused on task completion - for e.g., adjusting a returned draft, updating timelines, or shifting priorities without frustration. Volunteers for familiar tasks beyond immediate responsibilities, showing belief in their own abilities and willingness to take initiative, for e.g., offering to draft a basic note during internal reviews or compile data for a team report. Trusts personal judgment in new but simple situations and takes small, calculated steps without always seeking approval. For e.g., chooses to share a draft directly for review instead of waiting for peer validation.  Seeks support or alternate approaches when facing initial setbacks, persevering until tasks are completed successfully - for example, adapting a template to meet new requirements or consulting a peer for input on a revised draft.
Levels.Output_Description: I complete routine tasks with growing independence and take initiative when familiar with the work.  I listen to feedback and make corrections calmly, so my work improves and supports timely decisions.   I speak up when I’m confident, contribute to team discussions, and stay steady when challenges come up.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Completes multi-step assignments independently and adapts methods as needed, showing confidence in one’s ability to deliver reliably - for e.g., drafting inter-ministerial responses, finalising datasets, or preparing presentations for different audiences.   Applies sound judgment by selecting and adapting methods, tools, or formats based on what the task or audience requires - for e.g., using a simplified concept note for a district team or adjusting visual layouts for stakeholder briefings.  Contributes insights confidently in team settings by using analytical and communication skills to improve group outputs - for e.g., refining reporting formats, suggesting stronger slide structures, or flagging missing details in a joint presentation.  Evaluates personal strengths and limitations through reflection and feedback, and chooses assignments that build on current skill level - for e.g., leads documentation while observing planning tasks to grow steadily.  Approaches unfamiliar or ambiguous assignments with composure, breaks them into manageable steps, and seeks guidance only when necessary - for e.g., compiling inputs from multiple divisions for a policy brief or synthesising feedback from various departments.  Shares learnings from personal experiences, including past errors, to help team members build confidence and avoid common mistakes - for e.g., explaining how to fix recurring reporting errors or navigate challenging review processes.  Expresses well-reasoned views with respect and openness, challenges standard practices with reasoned suggestions, and helps the team improve quality or avoid risks - for e.g., recommending better routing processes, proposing streamlined templates, or suggesting agenda changes for efficiency.
Levels.Output_Description: I take ownership of new and complex tasks and complete them with confidence.  I share ideas or concerns in team settings and offer solutions when I observe something can be improved.  I learn from past work and feedback to handle challenges better and support others when needed.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Leads complex initiatives with assurance by applying prior experience and skills to deliver on strategic objectives, even when navigating ambiguity and institutional complexity - for e.g., overseeing the design and rollout of a flagship programme involving multiple ministries, coordinating cross-sector partnerships, or piloting a multi-state digital platform. Builds confidence in teams by mentoring peers and junior staff using personal lessons from complex or challenging work contexts - for e.g., demonstrating techniques to manage stakeholder pushback, navigate budget constraints, or present high-risk proposals convincingly. Drives bold, innovative reforms with conviction, justifying proposals through data, pilot learnings, and risk-mitigation strategies even in procedural-heavy environments - for e.g., initiating a digital grievance redressal pilot using AI within legacy systems. Maintains composure and ensures progress during institutional uncertainty, adapting plans while upholding core delivery goals - for e.g., restructuring implementation plans after funding delays, shifting strategies during leadership transitions, or redistributing resources mid-project. Fosters a psychologically safe work environment by encouraging open reflection and providing constructive feedback, enabling collective learning from high-pressure or uncertain situations - for e.g., conducting post-meeting debriefs, facilitating team reflection sessions, or sharing lessons learned after setbacks. Adapts leadership approach by recognising personal strengths and limitations, taking responsibility for areas where one adds the most value, and collaborating with others where specialised expertise is needed. For e.g., leads external engagement with district officials while coordinating technical inputs from domain experts.
Levels.Output_Description: I lead projects with clarity and confidence, even when things are uncertain or complex.  I stay steady during challenges, adapt to plans as needed, and help others feel safe and confident to do their best.  I help my team grow by sharing my experiences and using feedback and data to improve how we work.

Label: Active Listening
Description: Demonstrates the capacity to pay close attention to what is being said and to interpret the spirit and intent of the speaker from other cues such as body language and facial expressions.
Area Description: Exhibits the ability to speak, read, and interpret official communications such as English/Hindi/State-specific in respect to their context, message, and intent. Displays positive non-verbal cues, actively practices attentive listening, endeavours to understand the viewpoints of others, and responds respectfully and humbly. Presents information with conviction, fluency, rationality, and persuasion while using communication techniques tailored to the audience.

Label: Initiative and Drive
Description: Displays a profound drive to conduct oneself meticulously, following processes, norms, and other guidelines as one makes improvements by voluntarily and consciously putting forth the effort required by a task or activity despite difficulties, hardships, failures, or obstacles.
Area Description: Demonstrates an intense motivation to set goals and be responsible for their achievement while striving for quality excellence. Possesses the capacity to identify problems and gaps during service delivery and to proactively analyse and assess different options to ensure the best result. Drives systemic changes to increase effectiveness and efficiency in line with future needs.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Identifies and resolves common issues by referring to past examples and office guidelines (e.g., correcting format errors, tracking missing documents, etc) to minimise rework and reduce delays in processing. Uses standard templates, checklists, and notes to improve accuracy and reduce the need for supervisory corrections. Suggests minor process improvements during routine discussions (e.g., pointing out frequent errors, recommending simpler formats, etc) to support smoother operations and more efficient workflows. Responds positively to feedback and adapts approach in future tasks to avoid repeated mistakes and improve the quality of outputs. Delivers assigned tasks independently despite routine challenges (e.g., unclear instructions, data gaps, time pressure, etc) to maintain workflow continuity and uphold accountability. Acts on pressing work priorities without waiting for instructions by voluntarily initiating follow-ups or coordination to ensure timely file movement and service delivery.
Levels.Output_Description: I complete tasks by following templates and past circulars/office orders to reduce errors and ensure timely processing.  I improve my work by acting on feedback to reduce corrections and maintain accuracy.  I suggest improvements by pointing out routine issues to help make daily processes more efficient.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Analyses delays and inefficiencies (e.g., file movement, format inconsistencies, etc) to identify recurring bottlenecks and initiate corrective action for smoother operations. Proposes practical improvements (e.g., revised note formats, checklists, etc) aligned with departmental norms to improve accuracy, efficiency, and compliance. Applies feedback in coordination and follow-up by addressing recurring delays (e.g., incomplete inputs, missed deadlines) to strengthen the timeliness and reliability of outputs. Takes end-to-end ownership of broader assignments (e.g., preparing consolidated reports, coordinating cross-team inputs, etc) to ensure accountability and timely delivery. Coordinates across divisions by analysing recurring work priorities and addressing underlying delays (e.g., incomplete inputs, unclear responsibilities, etc) to maintain continuity and service responsiveness. Identifies and addresses emerging issues or service gaps (e.g., recurring citizen queries, workflow delays, etc) and proposes revised procedures or collaborations to enhance departmental efficiency. Adapts work plans and persists despite challenges (e.g., audit objections, missing documentation, etc) to deliver compliant and timely service outcomes.
Levels.Output_Description: I identify bottlenecks by analysing work patterns to suggest improvements that enhance team coordination and efficiency.  I take ownership of outcomes by coordinating with relevant teams to complete tasks on time and ensure accountability.  I overcome challenges by adapting my approach to find solutions that keep work moving despite delays.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Leads cross-functional efforts to resolve long-standing challenges (e.g., fragmented workflows, unclear delegation, etc) to improve service coordination and delivery effectiveness across the M/D/O. Drives systemic reforms by initiating policy, process, or workflow redesigns (e.g., SOPs, grievance redress protocols, etc) that address systemic gaps, strengthen preparedness, and reduce dependence on reactive responses. Champions cross-departmental pilots or process innovations (e.g., shared review logs, grievance dashboards, etc), ensuring successful practices are scaled and institutionalised to enhance timeliness. Promotes a culture of initiative by establishing mechanisms such as recognition systems, structured reflection cycles, or innovation platforms that reward experimentation and embed proactive behaviours across levels and teams.  Builds organisational capacity by designing structures (e.g., leadership pipelines, peer mentoring networks, targeted development programs, etc) to embed initiative and sustain a culture of ownership. Sustains momentum on challenging reforms despite resistance by advocating evidence-based solutions and modelling persistence to align innovation with organisational values and long-term goals.
Levels.Output_Description: I lead cross-functional efforts by resolving systemic challenges to improve coordination and delivery effectiveness across M/D/O.  I drive systemic reforms by initiating policy, process, or workflow redesigns to address emerging gaps, strengthen preparedness, and reduce reliance on reactive measures.  I promote a culture of initiative by creating mechanisms such as recognition systems, structured reflection cycles, or innovation platforms to reward experimentation and embed proactive behaviours across teams.

Label: Service Excellence wrt Citizens
Description: Demonstrating professional perseverance by focusing one’s efforts on meeting the set goals of the government as well as needs of the citizen while maintaining quality of delivery.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to focus one's efforts on discovering and meeting needs of stakeholder(s) by establishing a rapport, putting oneself into the mindset of the stakeholder and facilitating delivery of services that meet their underlying and unexpressed needs. Demonstrates the ability to provide excellent service delivery while developing and maintaining strong relationships with citizens and balancing the government's key strategic priorities.

Label: Self-Analysis
Description: Ability to critically examine and evaluate their own thoughts, behaviours, emotions, strengths, and weaknesses for introspection and self-reflection.
Area Description: Conscious recognition of one's thoughts, emotions, and behaviours, driven by a clear sense of purpose and values. It involves a confident understanding of one's strengths and weaknesses, fostering the ability to align actions with goals and a commitment to ongoing self-learning and personal growth.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Identifies personal emotions and thoughts in routine official situations to understand their influence on interactions and maintain professionalism in service delivery. Recognises how reflecting on one’s thoughts, emotions, and behaviour supports self-improvement and builds a respectful team environment and responsive service culture. Recognises individual strengths and limitations using self-observation or informal feedback, with the intention of performing duties more effectively and meeting service standards. Seeks and reflects on feedback regarding personal strengths, limitations, and workplace behaviour to make immediate adjustments in line with departmental norms and improve individual task performance. Recognises emotional reactions to everyday workplace challenges (e.g., task delays, disagreements) to regulate responses and maintain professional conduct. Reflects on induction learnings (e.g., code of conduct, service rules, service values) to align personal behaviour with departmental norms and uphold citizen service expectations through reliable and consistent delivery.
Levels.Output_Description: I recognise my emotions by reflecting on day-to-day work situations to improve my interactions and carry out responsibilities more effectively.  I act on feedback from supervisors or colleagues by adjusting my behaviour to align with departmental norms and maintain professional conduct.  I track my emotional responses across tasks to build self-awareness.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Reflects on recurring emotional patterns (e.g., frustration, defensiveness) during official interactions, such as interdepartmental coordination meetings, to recognise their impact on collaboration and efficiency. Adjusts personal conduct in response to recurring emotional triggers during tasks such as citizen grievance hearings or staff discussions to maintain composure, prevent conflict, and support a timely resolution. Applies insights from structured feedback mechanisms (e.g., performance reviews, supervisory discussions) to make sustained adjustments in behaviour, strengthen collaboration with colleagues, and improve reliability of task and service delivery. Applies self-reflection and emotional regulation strategies (e.g., taking pause, reframing perspective) to regulate emotions and maintain professionalism in high-pressure or sensitive tasks, including public-facing responsibilities. Modifies behavioural responses in line with departmental values and priorities to prevent delays, reduce disruptions, and ensure accountability in service delivery. Applies self-monitoring tools (e.g., reflection journals, behavioural checklists, etc) to recognise patterns in conduct and identify emotional triggers for consistent workplace effectiveness. Incorporates reflection into work routines (e.g., task debriefs, end-of-day logs) to track behavioural progress, reinforce learning, and strengthen long-term personal growth.
Levels.Output_Description: I analyse my behaviour by using feedback and self-assessment to strengthen my performance and contribute to smoother coordination within the team.  I manage my emotional responses by applying regulation strategies to stay composed and professional in high-pressure situations.  I adapt my actions by reflecting on past experiences to meet departmental expectations better and support consistent service delivery.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Leads by example through the consistent use of structured reflection tools (e.g., journaling, post-task reviews) to evaluate personal conduct, decision making, and emotional responses in complex or high-stakes situations, promoting a culture of accountability and professionalism. Mentors team members to identify emotional and behavioural patterns and apply self-regulation techniques (e.g., pausing before responding, identifying triggers), to reduce conflict, strengthen collaboration, and improve service delivery. Integrates reflective practices into planning, review, and team learning processes (e.g., staff meetings, feedback sessions, progress reviews, or performance discussions) to encourage professional growth, adaptive performance, and enhance self-awareness. Institutionalise systems for regular feedback and self-assessment (e.g., 360° feedback, peer reviews, team reflection practices) to promote behavioural improvement, reinforce accountability, and build a culture of openness and continuous learning across the organisation. Encourages use of reflective tools (e.g., Johari Window, peer reviews, surveys) to uncover blind spots, reinforce empathy, and prevent misuse of authority or bias in administrative decisions. Ensures alignment of individual and team behaviour with departmental mandates, service values, and citizen-centric goals to improve responsiveness, professionalism, and long-term impact.
Levels.Output_Description: I demonstrate integrity, emotional awareness, and sound judgment by practising structured reflection in complex situations.  I mentor my team to recognise and manage emotional patterns, fostering respectful collaboration and enhancing service delivery.  I institutionalise reflection by embedding tools such as 360° feedback, peer reviews, and reflection checklists in team routines to drive continuous improvement and citizen-centric outcomes.

Label: Purposefulness
Description: Ability to align their actions, decisions, and goals with a clear sense of purpose or a higher meaning for working towards the goal of greater good of citizens.
Area Description: Conscious recognition of one's thoughts, emotions, and behaviours, driven by a clear sense of purpose and values. It involves a confident understanding of one's strengths and weaknesses, fostering the ability to align actions with goals and a commitment to ongoing self-learning and personal growth.

Label: Navigating Ambiguity
Description: Ability to effectively operate in situations characterised by uncertainty, complexity, and a lack of clear information or direction by adapting quickly to changing circumstances, be they new policy directives, unexpected challenges, or shifts in political priorities.
Area Description: Recognises one's emotional triggers and controls emotional reactions for retaining professionalism while working under pressure and heightened stress. Shows perseverance, self-control, and professional toughness when facing challenges and recovering from setbacks by adapting attitude and behaviour to meet a particular circumstance or in response to shifting working conditions and dynamics. Shows adaptability in fulfilling a given responsibility, navigating through uncertain situations with limited knowledge or resources.

Label: Conflict Management
Description: Demonstrates the ability to anticipate, recognise, and resolve conflicts arising from differing opinions, objectives, or interests among individuals, teams, or external stakeholders. Applies structured assessment, negotiation, and mediation tools to de-escalate disputes, build consensus, and protect relationships, decisions, and delivery. Ensures that resolution processes are transparent, compliant, and procedurally fair, reinforcing organisational trust and accountability.
Area Description: Effectively builds alliances, facilitates negotiations, and aligns diverse stakeholder interests to drive collective decision-making. Applies diplomatic deliberation, conflict resolution, and consensus-building techniques to achieve impactful outcomes in alignment with national priorities.

Label: Stress Management
Description: Identifies one’s emotional triggers, regulates emotional responses through self-control, and maintains the dignity of the office and the position.
Area Description: Recognises one's emotional triggers and controls emotional reactions for retaining professionalism while working under pressure and heightened stress. Shows perseverance, self-control, and professional toughness when facing challenges and recovering from setbacks by adapting attitude and behaviour to meet a particular circumstance or in response to shifting working conditions and dynamics. Shows adaptability in fulfilling a given responsibility, navigating through uncertain situations with limited knowledge or resources.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Recognises personal emotional stress signals triggered by routine public service challenges, such as addressing public grievances or RTI case surges, and initiates basic calming techniques to sustain composure. Demonstrates self-control during minor stressors (for e.g., managing RTI applications, during feedback, interdepartmental coordination,  delays in meetings) and avoids reactive communication.  Applies basic stress reduction practices such as time management, prioritisation, or short breaks to manage daily pressures.  Seeks guidance from seniors or colleagues when overwhelmed, especially during reporting deadlines or last-minute demands. Utilises basic digital tools (e.g., calendar reminders, task lists, email flags, etc.) to organise workload and manage information, understanding how these aid in personal stress reduction.  Aware of one’s own feelings, strengths, and weaknesses, and their connection to one’s own actions and performance.
Levels.Output_Description: I maintain punctuality and deliver quality outcomes during high workload periods by managing my time effectively and seeking support when necessary.  I manage my emotions with self-awareness and maintain respectful communication to prevent misunderstandings.  I seek timely guidance from supervisors on managing stress to ensure service standards are upheld.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Provides guidance and peer mentoring on stress management during peak administrative periods like election deployments or audit preparations, facilitating workload adjustments and reinforcing team resilience Applies proven stress regulation strategies (e.g., breathing exercises, reframing, digital task delegation, etc.) during fluctuating workloads or stakeholder pressures. Anticipates team and personal stress triggers during high-pressure periods like elections, inspections, or tight budget cycles, and plans accordingly. Evaluates the impact of emotional dynamics on team productivity and morale, adjusting leadership or coordination approaches to maintain service quality. Uses project dashboards, workload monitoring tools (e.g., eOffice MIS, etc.), and regular check-ins to track team stress levels and rebalance work where needed. Actively manages self-effectiveness by thoroughly understanding one's own responses.
Levels.Output_Description: I ensure smooth and continuous operations by anticipating stress and proactively redistributing workloads.  I mentor colleagues by offering constructive feedback and emotional support to sustain team effectiveness after stressful events.  I foster a resilient team environment by recognizing early burnout signs, facilitating timely interventions or workload adjustments, and preventing delays in citizen-facing services.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Strengthens organisational stress resilience by integrating wellness and emotional regulation into strategic planning and team operations. Guides adoption of departmental wellness initiatives aligned with DoPT and workplace health guidelines (for e.g., well-being check-ins, SOPs for high-stress events, etc) to proactively mitigate chronic stressors. Ensures resilience frameworks meet compliance and audit standards (for e.g., audit backlash by CAG/CVC, major scheme rollout, etc.) to reduce institutional risk during oversight. Develops contingency plans and scheduling during major government scheme rollouts (PMAY, Digital India) to safeguard staff well-being and service quality. Fosters a psychologically safe work environment promoting ethical leadership, transparency, and collective perseverance.  Advocates for the use of AI and analytics tools to monitor workload imbalance and emotional risk factors in team performance data.
Levels.Output_Description: I embed departmental practices with government wellness policies to sustain citizen-centric service quality during crises and prolonged high-pressure events. I ensure compliance with audit and oversight standards related to staff well-being, reducing institutional risks and enhancing organizational credibility.  I champion transparent communication and ethical leadership during major programs, reinforcing public trust and sustaining staff morale through a culture of resilience

Label: Verbal and non-verbal fluency
Description: Demonstrates ability to articulate thoughts and express/present ideas clearly and effectively using oral, non-verbal communication skills, aiming to inform, instruct, or persuade the audience/ stakeholders, and augments credibility of the position and the office.
Area Description: Exhibits the ability to speak, read, and interpret official communications such as English/Hindi/State-specific in respect to their context, message, and intent. Displays positive non-verbal cues, actively practices attentive listening, endeavours to understand the viewpoints of others, and responds respectfully and humbly. Presents information with conviction, fluency, rationality, and persuasion while using communication techniques tailored to the audience.

Label: Responsiveness
Description: Demonstrate the ability to respond respectfully and effectively and ensure the highest level of care and satisfaction of citizens.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to focus one's efforts on discovering and meeting needs of stakeholder(s) by establishing a rapport, putting oneself into the mindset of the stakeholder and facilitating delivery of services that meet their underlying and unexpressed needs. Demonstrates the ability to provide excellent service delivery while developing and maintaining strong relationships with citizens and balancing the government's key strategic priorities.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Demonstrates basic awareness of one’s own role in citizen service delivery and follows defined service standards, escalation pathways, and response norms without deviation. Responds courteously and promptly to citizen/stakeholder requests, ensuring respect in tone and actions (for e.g., using approved response scripts, language templates, adhering to prescribed communication protocols, and keeping citizens informed of progress, etc). Documents queries and responses accurately in systems like CPGRAMS and e-Office to maintain transparency, while updating status regularly, mitigating citizen follow up.  Demonstrates empathy, especially in cases where citizens struggle to articulate their needs (for e.g., by asking clarifying questions, restating concerns to confirm understanding, etc). Escalates issues responsibly if beyond personal authority, ensuring no grievance is left unattended, while keeping citizens informed about expected timelines and next steps.  Refers to official guidelines, FAQs, Citizen Charters, and approved knowledge repositories to respond accurately, avoiding assumptions or unofficial advice. Proactively seeks clarification or support from supervisors or peers when faced with unfamiliar queries or situations, rather than delaying or providing incorrect information. Handles citizen information responsibly by adhering to data-sharing protocols and confidentiality norms during communication and documentation. Identifies simple recurring issues and flags them to supervisors (for e.g., repeated delays in a specific service, frequent documentation confusion, etc).
Levels.Output_Description: I respond to citizens respectfully and empathetically on time. I follow service standards, escalate when needed, and document all cases accurately to maintain transparency. I use official guidelines, seek support when unsure, protect citizen data, and flag recurring issues for improvement.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Analyses  dashboards and MIS tools (e.g., CPGRAMS, MyGov portal, etc) to track citizen satisfaction and systemic responsiveness by identifying patterns of delays, repeat complaints, and discontinuation of services.  Balances competing departmental priorities by applying structured coordination, negotiating realistic timelines and communicating proactively with internal and external stakeholders.  Analyses citizen narratives to distinguish between procedural issues, service gaps, and emotional reassurance needs, and responds accordingly.  Demonstrates emotional regulation and composure when managing complex, sensitive, or escalated citizen interactions, maintaining professionalism under pressure.  Demonstrates sensitivity to vulnerable or marginalised groups and adapts engagement to reduce power asymmetries and discomfort.  Improves procedures where delays or repeated complaints indicate deeper service gaps by applying root cause / problem tree analysis tools (for e.g., 5 Whys, Fishbone diagrams, etc) to recommend fixes.  Mentors team members on treating every citizen interaction as an opportunity to build trust and models appropriate tone, empathy, and professionalism.  Coordinates across sections/departments (for e.g., Finance, IT, field offices) to resolve cross-cutting issues promptly (for eg., approvals, system-errors, verification, and policy conflicts) and provide efficient service to citizens.  Establishes feedback loops (for e.g., post-service calls, IVRS surveys, WhatsApp check-ins) to validate if citizen needs were truly met.  Ensures communication is adapted to diverse audiences (language, literacy, accessibility) using plain-language guidelines and translated materials.  Monitors grievances and proactively intervenes when cases risk breaching service delivery timelines.
Levels.Output_Description: I use dashboards and feedback to understand citizen needs, identify service gaps, and ensure timely, accurate responses.  I coordinate across teams, and resolve cross-cutting issues so citizens receive smooth and reliable services.  I communicate with empathy, adapt to diverse citizens, stay composed under pressure, and mentor others to uphold high service standards.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Drives and ensures responsiveness frameworks (e.g., service-level standards, grievance-redressal protocols, citizen charter mechanisms) are embedded in Ministry/Departmental priorities. Enforces clear service standards by strengthening Citizen Charters, defining measurable service delivery benchmarks, and ensuring these are publicly communicated, periodically updated, and rigorously monitored. Sets norms for transparency through public dashboards and citizen feedback loops to strengthen accountability and continuous improvement in citizen service delivery. Leads teams by demonstrating and reinforcing best practices in active listening, empathetic communication, emotional regulation, and professional tonality, ensuring that staff consistently interact with citizens respectfully and deliver services effectively. Creates enabling ecosystems for responsiveness by strengthening institutional capabilities, improving grievance platforms, building staff competencies, and ensuring resources, SOPs, and digital systems are continuously upgraded for better delivery of services to the citizens. Influences organisational mindsets by role-modelling responsiveness, inspiring teams to prioritise citizen needs, and promoting a culture where officials take ownership of outcomes, not just processes. Balances long-term national priorities with immediate service responsiveness (for e.g., ensuring continuity of policy commitments while simultaneously mobilising rapid citizen support during crises or emergencies). Creates a culture where officials at all levels value and practice responsiveness, by modelling desired behaviours, institutionalising service standards, integrating responsiveness into training and appraisal systems.
Levels.Output_Description: I embed and uphold robust service standards and responsiveness frameworks  through clear benchmarks, dashboards, and citizen feedback mechanisms.  I build a culture of accountability and service excellence by mentoring teams and strengthening institutional capabilities.  I balance long-term priorities with immediate citizen needs, strengthening systems and driving timely, reliable service delivery across the institution.

Label: Planning and Prioritisation
Description: Demonstrates the ability to effectively manage tasks and reorder priorities in order to make the most use of available resources.
Area Description: Establishes plans, defines milestones, and allots resources for regularly producing high-quality, cost-effective results that are in accordance with the department's or ministry's overall objectives. Focuses on tasks that will increase effectiveness, efficiency, and production through planning, intentional effort, and control. Determines goals, assigns tasks, and establishes priorities based on skill levels and the availability of resources.

Label: Diversity and Inclusion
Description: Create an inclusive work environment that values and embraces diverse perspectives, backgrounds, and experiences by actively promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) within the Ministry/Department/Organization and integrating it into various aspects of working.
Area Description: Working in tandem with others as an integral unit, establishing partnerships and cooperative working relationships toward realizing Division / Department / Ministry goals. Proactively sharing relevant and useful knowledge, experience, or expertise with peers/team to strengthen knowledge base. It also refers to leveraging diverse strengths and contribution of every officer/stafff or timely and high-quality delivery.

Label: Sharing Constructive Feedback
Description: Provides timely feedback in a helpful, supportive, and constructive manner to others in a way that promotes their personal and professional growth while maintaining a positive and respectful working relationship.
Area Description: Fosters a positive work environment by actively engaging with the team, enabling their growth and enhancing their proficiency. This involves identifying and creating opportunities to enhance officers’ skills and competencies through timely feedback, guidance, and mentoring. Additionally, promoting a culture of trust, openness, appreciation, recognition, parity, and leading by example.

Label: Goal Setting
Description: Displays an underlying desire to be thorough and accurate when determining clear, challenging, & SMART (specific, measurable, achievable, relevant & time-bound) objectives or targets to guide actions as per set procedures, rules & guidelines.
Area Description: Demonstrates an intense motivation to set goals and be responsible for their achievement while striving for quality excellence. Possesses the capacity to identify problems and gaps during service delivery and to proactively analyse and assess different options to ensure the best result. Drives systemic changes to increase effectiveness and efficiency in line with future needs.

Label: Commitment to Quality
Description: Facilitates building a culture of delivering high-quality outputs through consistent focus on creating new benchmarks in quality and maintaining them for both internal and external stakeholders.
Area Description: Demonstrates an intense motivation to set goals and be responsible for their achievement while striving for quality excellence. Possesses the capacity to identify problems and gaps during service delivery and to proactively analyse and assess different options to ensure the best result. Drives systemic changes to increase effectiveness and efficiency in line with future needs.

Label: Continuous Improvement
Description: Demonstrate the ability to deliver high-quality services through improvement of existing processes, systems, and practices within the sections, divisions, or departments.
Area Description: Establishes plans, defines milestones, and allots resources for regularly producing high-quality, cost-effective results that are in accordance with the department's or ministry's overall objectives. Focuses on tasks that will increase effectiveness, efficiency, and production through planning, intentional effort, and control. Determines goals, assigns tasks, and establishes priorities based on skill levels and the availability of resources.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Demonstrates ability to follow prescribed rules and instructions (for e.g., DoPT guidelines, CCS Rules, office manuals, etc) by referring to established procedures before acting, ensuring that work is compliant and free from avoidable errors.  Shows understanding of existing office systems and workflows by observing how files, notes, and approvals move, thereby developing a foundational awareness of how the section operates.  Uses digital platforms (for e.g., e-Office, SPARROW, CPGRAMS, GeM, etc), ensuring tasks are processed in the correct systems and remain trackable and transparent.  Maintains accuracy in file noting and drafting under supervision by presenting facts correctly, following templates, and checking for errors, contributing to clearer documentation and less rework.  Identifies small or obvious inefficiencies (for e.g., duplicate paperwork, unnecessary movement of files, delays in obtaining signatures, etc) and brings them to the notice of the supervisor, contributing to smoother daily operations.  Follows required formats, templates, and quality norms when preparing drafts or inputs, helping maintain consistency across the section’s outputs.  Seeks clarification proactively when instructions, rules, or processes are unclear, demonstrating responsibility for accuracy and reducing mistakes.  Shows readiness to support guided improvements (for e.g., adopting a new step in the workflow, simplifying a repetitive task, etc), helping improve quality while learning from supervisors.
Levels.Output_Description: I complete tasks accurately and within prescribed timelines using established rules and systems.  I use approved digital platforms to ensure transparency and traceability.  I identify and report simple inefficiencies that affect daily section operations.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Improves end-to-end workflows (for e.g., RTI routing process, procurement approval cycle, grievance redressal chain, etc) and removes redundant approval layers to reduce TAT.  Analyses MIS dashboards and pendency reports (e.g., CPGRAMS pendency dashboard, e-Office disposal reports, PFMS fund release tracking) to identify bottlenecks.  Ensures alignment with government initiatives (for e.g., Digital India compliance, Sevottam service standards, Citizen Charter commitments, etc) while redesigning workflows. Conducts structured root-cause analysis (for e.g., cause-effect mapping for repeated audit objections, process mapping for delayed sanction approvals, etc) to diagnose inefficiencies.  Uses stakeholder feedback (for e.g., citizen grievance categories in CPGRAMS, RTI query trends, audit observations, etc) to refine internal processes. Introduces pilot improvements (for e.g., digitising manual registers, simplifying internal checklists, introducing weekly pendency tracker sheets, etc) before full implementation. Designs monitoring tools (for e.g., Excel-based pendency dashboards, colour-coded compliance trackers, weekly review templates, etc) to enhance accountability. Measures impact by comparing pre- and post-intervention indicators (for e.g., file disposal time reduced from 15 to 9 days, grievance closure rate improved by 20%, etc)  Improves accessibility by simplifying document requirements (for e.g., reducing unnecessary affidavit submissions, standardising checklist-based applications).
Levels.Output_Description: I streamline workflows to reduce delays, duplication, and resource wastage.  I use data and feedback to improve service reliability and accountability.  I monitor measurable performance indicators to sustain efficiency improvements.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Anticipates systemic inefficiencies (for e.g., recurring inter-departmental approval delays, repeated audit paras, etc) and proposes structural reforms aligned with national priorities (for e.g., Ease of Living, Digital Governance initiatives, etc).  Creates M/D/O wide dashboards tracking pendency, TAT, cost-per-case, grievance timelines, and compliance metrics using MIS or BI tools.
