Provides suggestions regarding automation and analytics integration (for e.g., auto-escalation triggers in e-Office, AI-enabled grievance categorisation, dashboard alerts for overdue files, etc) to reduce manual bottlenecks.  Benchmarks departmental processes against peer Ministries, best-performing States, or Sevottam standards to drive improvement.  Creates new models, tools, or frameworks to embed improvements, for example designing a simple root-cause analysis toolkit for all divisions to use. Establishes quarterly efficiency and performance review mechanisms (for e.g., pendency review meetings chaired by HoD, dashboard-based review sessions, etc) to sustain long-term improvements. Builds transparency mechanisms (for e.g., publishing service timelines on department website, public grievance dashboards, etc) to strengthen citizen trust. Leads cross-department process harmonisation (for e.g., standardising RTI routing across divisions, integrating grievance tracking across multiple portals, etc). Ensures structured change management (for e.g., circulating rollout notes, FAQs, capacity building sessions for revised workflows, etc) to institutionalise reforms.
Levels.Output_Description: I lead system-wide reforms that improve efficiency, transparency, and cost-effectiveness.  I institutionalise technology-enabled monitoring systems to sustain long-term improvements.  I align process reforms with citizen-centric service standards and national governance priorities.

Label: Systems Thinking
Description: Recognize one's area of responsibility as a critical component of the larger system of the Government machinery and understand its interrelatedness and interoperability with other areas. This involves perceiving and comprehending complex problems and systems, such as processes and policies, as a whole and in parts simultaneously. It also involves devising strategies that would impact the dynamics of the whole by synthesizing complex problems, creating feedback loops, and comprehending causality.
Area Description: Displays a sense of carefulness and practicality in employing both creative and reflective problem-solving methods. Conducts thorough root-cause analyses and collaborates with experts and stakeholders to generate a range of hypotheses based on historical precedents, data, reports, with a goal to anticipate potential issues and suggest practical and alternative solutions that are based on insights gained from a comprehensive analysis of all relevant available information.

Label: Resource Management
Area Description: Establishes plans, defines milestones, and allots resources for regularly producing high-quality, cost-effective results that are in accordance with the department’s or ministry’s overall objectives. Focuses on tasks that will increase effectiveness, efficiency, and production through planning, intentional effort, and control. Determines goals, assigns tasks, and establishes priorities based on skill levels and the availability of resources.

Label: Transformation Orientation
Description: Ability to drive cultural, attitudinal, and operational transformation within a Department or Ministry is demonstrated by fostering innovative thinking that utilises emerging technological and digital advances, while also mitigating potential risks and capitalising on opportunities to enhance governance efficiency.
Area Description: Champions ‘out-of-the-box’ thinking, and supports experimentation of new ideas, utilizing technological advancements with the aim of transforming operations to enhance governance efficiency. Synthesizes insights and concepts from diverse disciplines to drive the decision-making process, promote wider acceptance, and encourage cooperation to ensure successful implementation.

Label: Empathy
Description: Ability to understand and connect with the emotions, perspectives, and feelings of others and take actions to support them through challenging situations.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to focus one's efforts on discovering and meeting the needs of stakeholders by establishing a rapport, putting oneself into the mindset of the stakeholder and facilitating the delivery of services that meet their underlying and unexpressed needs. Demonstrates the ability to provide excellent service delivery while developing and maintaining strong relationships with citizens and balancing the government's key strategic priorities.

Label: Global Mindset
Description: Demonstrates the ability to enhance Departmental processes aligned to global standards by staying well-versed with the evolving dynamics at the global level, including those related to geo-politics, economics, society, and technology. Understands and keeps up-to-date on international policies and trends that affect the organisation and shape stakeholders' views.
Area Description: Ability to foresee future challenges and identify opportunities by discerning the operating environment holistically and being cognizant of emerging dynamics and trends globally, locally, and internally. Creates and communicates a vision of success, coupled with a progressive outlook and aligning strategic goals with operational plans to achieve the vision. Exhibits prudence, credibility and authority for shaping strategic direction to achieve Department/Ministry mandates.

Label: Reading and Comprehension
Description: Exhibits elevated reading skills, possesses a firm linguistic command, and knowledge. Demonstrates the ability to absorb information, understand it, and integrate it with existing knowledge.
Area Description: Exhibits the ability to speak, read, and interpret official communications such as English/Hindi/State-specific in respect to their context, message, and intent. Displays positive non-verbal cues, actively practices attentive listening, endeavours to understand the viewpoints of others, and responds respectfully and humbly. Presents information with conviction, fluency, rationality, and persuasion while using communication techniques tailored to the audience.

Label: Forward Thinking
Description: Demonstrates the ability to comprehend the internal and external environmental dynamics and their effect on both the short-term goals and long-term mandates of the Department/ Ministry/ Organization. This entails achieving a strategic balance with multiple stakeholders on sensitive issues while maintaining a clear focus on the mandate of the future for pro-people governance.
Area Description: Ability to foresee future challenges and identify opportunities by discerning the operating environment holistically and being cognizant of emerging dynamics and trends globally, locally, and internally. Creates and communicates a vision of success, coupled with a progressive outlook and aligning strategic goals with operational plans to achieve the vision. Exhibits prudence, credibility and authority for shaping strategic direction to achieve Department/Ministry mandates.

Label: Sustainable Mindset
Description: Exhibits a commitment to addressing the Department’s, Ministry’s, or Organisation’s needs now and, in the future, through focusing on learning by identifying one’s own learning needs, setting a learning plan and choosing the appropriate learning resources. Focuses on learning how new and innovative ideas and emerging technologies can be adopted to contribute towards the successful completion of tasks.
Area Description: Conscious recognition of one’s thoughts, emotions, and behaviours, driven by a clear sense of purpose and values. It involves a confident understanding of one’s strengths and weaknesses, fostering the ability to align actions with goals and a commitment to ongoing self-learning and personal growth.

Label: Inspiring Others
Description: Demonstrates the characteristics and traits of Karmayogis, including fulfilling commitments on every task, recognising, appreciating, and encouraging each team member, stakeholder, and partner, and staying true to the principles of good governance, and encourages others in inculcating them. Displaying a high level of emotional intelligence and being alert, careful, organized, and diligent for setting the right example for the team.
Area Description: Fosters a positive work environment by actively engaging with the team, enabling their growth, and enhancing their proficiency. This involves identifying and creating opportunities to enhance officers' skills and competencies through timely feedback, guidance, and mentoring. Additionally, promoting a culture of trust, openness, appreciation, recognition, parity, and leading by example.

Label: Executive Presence
Description: Exhibits a blend of professional charismatic persona that exudes calmness, composure, confidence, effective communication, and commanding presence in the room while engaging in inter-Ministerial, National and Global forums, intending to promote Government Programmes, Schemes and Strategies.
Area Description: Ability to foresee future challenges and identify opportunities by discerning the operating environment holistically and being cognizant of emerging dynamics and trends globally, locally, and internally. Creates and communicates a vision of success, coupled with a progressive outlook and aligns strategic goals with operational plans to achieve the vision. Exhibits prudence, credibility and authority for shaping strategic direction to achieve Department/Ministry mandates.

Label: Digital Tools (MS office, Excel, PPT & AI tools) & Platforms
Description: Utilises digital tools, systems, platforms, Microsoft Office, and software for promoting e-Governance both inter- and intra-departmentally within the Ministry's ecosystem. Demonstrates vigilance in safeguarding data and sensitive information from potential risks and unauthorised access, including inspection, modification, recording, disruption, or destruction by internal or external individuals/agencies.
Area Description: Understands, identifies, implements, and utilises digital tools for accurate, speedy, and effective delivery for superior visualisation and decision-making at different levels of the organisation. Leverages digital technologies and platforms to collaborate effectively and efficiently with multiple stakeholders.

Label: Budget Formulation and Implementation
Description: Demonstrates the ability to forecast and prepare budget for Division / Department / Ministry / Organisation, for specified period, aligned to the guidelines and standards formulated by Ministry of Finance
Area Description: Formulates budget and expenditure plans aligned to the objectives and budget allocation of the Ministry / Department / Organization, in accordance with the rules and guidelines of the Ministry of Finance, while also forecasting future expenditures. Manages and monitors the Division/ Department's budget, accounts & expenditure, while promoting financial prudence within the Department to enhance its overall efficiencies. Develops proficiency in handling PFMS (Public Financial Management System)

Label: Digital Service Design
Description: Develops and executes services with a citizen-centric approach, leveraging efficient and effective touchpoints, involving end-users in iterative development, assessment, and utilising digital technologies for periodic enhancements based on feedback and learning. Proactively manages change by mitigating risks associated with digital technology, navigating barriers in digital transformation, and ensuring sustainability within the government
Area Description: Understands, identifies, implements, and utilises digital tools for accurate, speedy, and effective delivery for superior visualisation and decision-making at different levels of the organisation. Leverages digital technologies and platforms to collaborate effectively and efficiently with multiple stakeholders.

Label: Office Procedures
Description: Demonstrates proficiency in office administration, file and record management, and adherence to standard operating procedures to ensure the seamless functioning of government departments. This includes managing official correspondence, workflow coordination, document processing and leveraging digital tools for efficiency, compliance and transparency in governance.
Area Description: Understands, applies and optimises office procedures for effective documentation, structured decision-making, timely service delivery and accountability. Leverages digital office management tools, ensures compliance with government regulations, and facilitates streamlined interdepartmental coordination for enhanced efficiency and citizen service.

Label: Project Planning
Description: Define various aspects of project including creating proposal, defining objectives, deliverables, timelines, intended benefits, mode of project (PPP, BOOT etc.), costing, funding schedule, technology, and process for selecting consultants, procurement, advisors, stakeholder management framework, reporting, monitoring, assessment, knowledge management mechanism etc.
Area Description: Demonstrates ability to plan phases/stages and identify key activities, dependencies, and milestones for driving projects. Utilises & creates various standard framework / mechanisms to determine project mode, vendor selection & engagement, implementation, communication, knowledge transition, and risk mitigation. Evaluates progress against KPIs and ensures adherence to relevant guidelines & policies etc. for ensuring optimisation of resource utilisation, and driving realisation of intended benefit for the intended audience.

Label: Research and Need Analysis
Description: Demonstrates the ability to undertake primary and secondary research on a variety of aspects. Analyses and assesses a range of data points and reports produced by or obtained from a spectrum of sources (including but not limited to the subject of complaints, proposals by stakeholders, a range of appropriate indices, reports by governmental/non-governmental agencies, and gaps indicated in the course of interpretation and/or implementation).
Area Description: Demonstrates the capacity to detect gaps and improvement needs in existing policies in order to translate policy concepts into policy formulation for addressing existing and emerging needs of the citizens. Conducts thorough study and analysis of best practices at State, National, or Global levels, takes use of modern innovation and the most recent advancements in the social, economic, technological, and legal spheres while creating & implementing policies. Focuses on monitoring the application of policy and evaluating its impact and efficacy using a variety of instruments for responsible, open, and sustainable governance.

Label: Data Management
Description: Demonstrates the ability to collect and organize data, ensure data quality through data cleansing, protect data, store it, and transform it into usable and appropriate formats for warehousing, analyzing, and decision-making purposes.
Area Description: Collects, compiles, and assesses data, information, and knowledge resources in appropriate formats to enable interpretation and analysis for evidence-based decision-making. Scrutinizes data and reports with a keen eye for detail, evaluates their accuracy, relevance, trends, and anomalies, extracts insights, and contextualizes and critically analyses key points to develop data-driven strategic proposals and decisions while being mindful of prescribed rules & processes of data-handling within the government.

Label: Data-led Decision Making
Description: Utilises data analytics to extract insights, present compelling perspectives, and make strategic decisions for the efficient disposal of business, improved citizen experience, and streamlining operations to fulfill the Department's mandate.
Area Description: Collects, compiles, and assesses data, information, and knowledge resources in appropriate formats to enable interpretation and analysis for evidence-based decision-making. Scrutinises data and reports with a keen eye for detail, evaluates their accuracy, relevance, trends, and anomalies, extracts insights, and contextualises and critically analyses key points to develop data-driven strategic proposals and decisions while being mindful of prescribed rules & processes of data-handling within the government.

Label: Noting and Drafting of Official Communications
Description: Demonstrates the ability to concisely summarize the key aspects of a proposal or document under consideration, ensuring accuracy in facts, statistics, and relevant rulings. Conducts thorough fact-checking to support informed decision-making by government officers. Capable of drafting preliminary versions of official communications, incorporating necessary details for clarity and precision, to be finalized upon approval by the concerned officer.
Area Description: Administration and enforcement of compliance in accordance with the most recent "Central Secretariat Manual of Office Procedures" (CSMOP) to ensure that the Section, Division, or Department operates effectively and efficiently. Understands the issues and analyses precedence, facts, ruling position, etc., and records remarks and proposes actions for enabling competent authority to review and make decisions for timely disposal. Concisely drafts written correspondence such as O.M., memos, notices, reports, letters, e-mails, etc. to be issued on approval of the competent authority. Articulates all kinds of communications towards administration, policy administration, implementation, advisory, reporting, proposal writing, and any other documentation towards handling departmental matters with focus on good governance.

Label: Technical Proposal/Report Writing
Description: Creates reports / proposals clearly stating objective, need, supporting facts, various parameters, observations, conclusions, and recommendations depending on the requirement of the Department / Ministry / Mission etc.
Area Description: Administration and enforcement of compliance in accordance with the most recent "Central Secretariat Manual of Office Procedures" (CSMOP) to ensure that the Section, Division, or Department operates effectively and efficiently. Understands the issues and analyses precedence, facts, ruling position etc., and records remarks and proposes actions for enabling competent authority to review and make decisions for timely disposal. Concisely drafts written correspondence such as O.M., memos, notices, reports, letters, e-mails etc. to be issued on approval of competent authority. Articulates all kinds of communications towards administration, policy administration, implementation, advisory, reporting, proposal writing and any other documentation towards handling departmental matters with focus on good governance.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Understands the structure of technical proposals such as Request for Proposals (RFPs), Concept Notes, and Grant Proposals, ensuring inclusion of key components like objectives, scope, evaluation criteria, and submission requirements.  Articulates the purpose, scope, and expected outcomes of a proposal or report to align with departmental priorities.  Gathers quantitative and qualitative data from field visits, surveys, performance monitoring dashboards, or official reports to support proposal justifications.  Drafts proposals/ reports in standardised formats and templates clearly outlining objectives, needs, supporting facts, observations, conclusions, and recommendations based on departmental or mission-specific guidelines.  Classifies observations/ findings/ content logically, highlighting patterns, gaps, and trends using structured sections, bullet points, tables, charts, and case studies where necessary to enhance clarity of the proposal/ reports.  Demonstrates basic proficiency in document processing tools like Google Docs and MS Word for drafting clear and well-structured reports, while utilising tools such as Excel, Tableau to create graphs, pie charts, and other visuals to present supporting facts and data in a visually compelling and easy-to-understand way.  Incorporates feedback from supervisors and stakeholders, refining content based on evaluation criteria.  Ensures timely submission of reports and proposals, adhering to designated review cycles and approval hierarchies.
Levels.Output_Description: I understand the purpose and scope of the proposal/ report and gather data and information to support the drafting process  I draft structured proposals and reports that align with departmental priorities and follow standardised templates, ensuring logical flow of content.  I incorporate feedback from supervisors and stakeholders to refine content and adhere to submission timelines.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Develops and disseminates structured frameworks for reports and proposals that adhere to established formats, ensuring consistency, standardisation, and compliance with official guidelines.  Assigns roles and responsibilities to team members, ensuring tasks are clearly defined, timelines are outlined, and work is distributed based on expertise and available resources, fostering clarity, ownership, and adherence to quality standards of accuracy, coherence, and completeness before final approval and submission.  Oversees the collection, verification, and synthesis of data from various credible sources (field reports, monitoring dashboards, performance evaluations) to substantiate findings, ensuring data integrity and accuracy.  Reviews and refines technical proposals and reports by applying quality control measures to ensure they articulate objectives, needs, data-driven facts, and insights in a logical flow, aligning with departmental goals and adhering to the required formats and standards before submitting them for approval by higher authorities.  Creates internal and external review structures to facilitate consultations with relevant stakeholders at appropriate stages, validating key observations, refining recommendations, and ensuring alignment with organisational goals for effective proposal/report development.  Ensures timely adherence to submission timelines and procedural requirements, maintaining transparency and documentation throughout the proposal/report preparation process to facilitate effective tracking and accountability.
Levels.Output_Description: I develop and disseminate structured frameworks for reports and proposals, ensuring compliance with official guidelines and  objectives.  I oversee the review and refinement of proposals and reports, ensuring clarity, logical flow, data accuracy, and adherence to quality standards before submission.  I assign roles, define timelines, and distribute responsibilities within the team, ensuring adherence to submission deadlines and quality benchmarks.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Oversees the development and guides the creation of high-value technical proposals for securing funding, partnerships, international collaboration, development projects, intergovernmental agreements, in alignment to national priorities.  Coordinates and facilitates collaboration within interdepartmental teams to refine proposals/reports, ensuring input from all relevant stakeholders (government departments, experts, and external partners)   Reviews and assesses financial and administrative implications of the proposals/ reports to enhance policy influence, secure partnerships, and ensure compliance with national and international frameworks  Builds a pool of subject matter experts to strengthen the review mechanism of high value reports/ proposals ensuring strategic alignment with organisational goals, refining recommendations, and validating critical insights to enhance decision-making and impact.  Champions the adoption of global best practices and governance standards while proposal/ report writing.  Embeds procedures/structures to disseminate and consume findings and recommendations from reports and proposals across ministries, think tanks, and international platforms to enhance visibility, and inform policymaking.
Levels.Output_Description: I oversee the development of high-value proposals and reports, ensuring alignment with national priorities and policy influence to secure funding and strategic partnerships. I facilitate coordination between interdepartmental teams and external partners to integrate diverse experts inputs, to refine recommendations, and validate insights in the reports/ proposals.  I champion the adoption of global governance standards and establish structured mechanisms to disseminate reports to enhance visibility, inform policymaking, and drive strategic influence.

Label: E-Office
Description: Utilises the digital workplace designed to enable simplified, responsive, effective, and transparent working of all government offices, promotes collaboration, and facilitates effective knowledge sharing in the ecosystem.
Area Description: Administration and enforcement of compliance in accordance with the most recent "Central Secretariat Manual of Office Procedures" (CSMOP) to ensure that the Section, Division, or Department operates effectively and efficiently. Understands the issues and analyses precedence, facts, ruling position etc., and records remarks and proposes actions for enabling competent authority to review and make decisions for timely disposal. Concisely drafts written correspondence such as O.M., memos, notices, reports, letters, e-mails etc. to be issued on approval of competent authority. Articulates all kinds of communications towards administration, policy administration, implementation, advisory, reporting, proposal writing and any other documentation towards handling departmental matters with focus on good governance.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Understands the purpose of e-Office in enabling transparency, increasing accountability, reducing paper, tracking digital communication, and facilitating quick decision making.   Demonstrates a basic understanding of e-Office’s digital tools and platforms such as eFile, SPARROW, Knowledge Management System, etc to perform day-to-day activities (for e.g., opening assigned DAK, viewing file movement history, etc) for efficiency and transparency in alignment with the Central Secretariat Manual of Office Procedures (CSMOP) and Digital India goals.  Navigates core modules such as Inbox, Receipts and File View to access files, correspondence, and draft notes for daily tasks such as forwarding dak or viewing previous approvals.  Uploads scanned documents or attachments (for e.g., grievance letters, incoming dak, signed approvals, meeting records, etc)  to existing e-files using standard formats such as PDFs and simple naming conventions.  Recognises and follows step-by-step instructions for digital signing, file classification, and reference tagging for procedural accuracy and strengthening institutional memory.  Forwards receipts and files to designated officers based on assigned workflow, typically after inward diarisation or scanning of dak (for e.g., for pension applications or interdepartmental letters).  Utilises basic search functions to retrieve recently handled or directly assigned files, facilitating collaboration by sharing relevant information with supervisors and team members to support timely and informed decision making. Maintains workflow continuity and operational efficiency by reporting technical issues or system errors to supervisors.
Levels.Output_Description: I understand the core purpose of e‑Office to promote transparency and efficiency, and I use its basic modules to perform daily tasks in line with government protocols and Digital India goals.  I can perform essential digital actions and use simple search functions to retrieve or forward files, helping maintain continuity, reduce delays, and ensure proper documentation. I follow standard procedures using approved formats, and seek support from supervisors or colleagues, contributing to a collaborative and compliant digital workflow within my section or department.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Creates new e-files and sub-files by selecting appropriate classification, subject headings, and file types (for e.g., when initiating a file on scheme guidelines, circulars, or public grievances).   Drafts notes, letters, or memos directly in e-Office, embedding attachments such as previous orders, meeting records, or reference documents, and circulates these for approval using digital workflows.  Applies digital signature certificates (DSC) for self or supervisors to secure documents before dispatch, including sanction orders, RTI replies, or policy communication.  Uses advanced features such as linking related files, attaching reference documents, and performing targeted searches using metadata or subject lines for better decision-making.  Reviews and performs quality checks on e-files prepared by juniors, ensuring correct formats, proper labelling (e.g., Dak ID, subject line, date), and alignment with section/department practices.  Monitors pendency and progress of files using status views and takes follow-up actions such as sending e-Reminders, marking urgency, or following up on pending cases (for e.g., delays in RTI response, recruitment approvals, or finance clearances), ensuring accountability at every level.   Guides reportees or peers on standard practices such as receipt diarisation, attaching documents properly, or using correct formats and templates, fostering collaboration.  Documents FAQs for recurring queries (e.g., DSC activation steps, dealing with signature mismatches, use of TOC, etc), contributing to team-level readiness and internal knowledge management and continuity.  Flags recurring operational issues (e.g., system lags, signature errors, missing file links) and suggests minor workarounds or submits support requests to technical teams.  Coordinates tactical digitisation efforts within the section by ensuring current files are created digitally and by tracking progress on physical file migration.
Levels.Output_Description: I execute complex digital tasks while guiding peers in the correct use of workflows, proactively tracking pendency to reduce delays and improve service delivery.  I support digital governance and transparency by monitoring file pendency, tracking decision timelines using file histories, and flagging delays to ensure my section stays aligned with service delivery timelines and accountability frameworks.   I contribute to knowledge sharing by mentoring colleagues on best practices and ensuring consistent application of the e‑Office Manual across my team’s work.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Uses file movement analytics and MIS dashboards to identify bottlenecks, improving systemic turnaround time across departments. Streamlines digital workflows for recruitment, grievance redressal, or scheme implementation, ensuring use of templates, version control, and indexing features like TOC (Table of Contents). Leads efforts to digitise physical records by standardising scanning protocols, metadata tagging, and bundling protocols across diverse record types (audit reports, pension reports, disciplinary proceedings, etc) ensuring they are searchable and compliant with e-Office norms.  Supports peer learning or structured training and capacity building sessions on advanced e‑Office functionalities like managing inter-instance file transfers, tagging and linking correspondence to parent files, managing receipts from external platforms like CPGRAMS or eSamikSha, etc.  Drives inter-departmental knowledge sharing and reuse by promoting use of searchable indexes, version-controlled document histories, and e‑Office dashboards for analysing how past decisions (e.g., procurement approvals, vigilance inquiries, or stakeholder consultations) were handled, showcasing best practices in digital documentation and compliance, enabling more informed, consistent, and transparent governance. Ensures compliance with larger digital governance mandates such as the Digital India Mission, RTI transparency, e‑Governance guidelines, record retention schedules, or Sevottam delivery standards, by embedding traceability and timestamped documentation in digital workflows for scheme approvals, financial sanctions, policy drafts, or citizen services. Collaborates with NIC, DG Systems, or internal IT units by providing field-level feedback on functionality gaps or user issues to inform, enhance, and pilot feature upgrades (such as automated mail acknowledgements for dak receipts, SMS alerts for file escalation or movement, feedback loops to improve e‑Office accessibility, etc) to strengthen the effectiveness, responsiveness, and scalability of e‑Office implementation across M/D/Os.  Aligns e‑Office implementation with broader administrative reforms, such as transitioning grievance systems from paper to fully digital mode, integrating CPGRAMS or GSTN dashboards with e‑Office tracking, etc.
Levels.Output_Description: I lead efforts to institutionalise digital workflows, reducing duplication, and strengthening institutional memory.  I champion strategic use of e‑Office to support governance reforms.  I enable organisational learning by building searchable digital repositories, curating past precedents, and conducting training sessions - ensuring knowledge is captured, reused, and shared across teams for long-term administrative continuity.

Label: Conduct Rules
Description: Understands the extent, applicability, intent, and content of the Central Civil Services (Conduct) Rules, 1964 (updated) for Government servants with a view to maintain integrity in public services by taking required action to promote its adherence.
Area Description: Demonstrates a thorough understanding and practical application of administrative vigilance and disciplinary procedures, which serve as safeguards to ensure the proper implementation of government processes, policies, and rules, while identifying instances of breach by government employees who may abuse their authority or power of approval for personal gain. Facilitates in building a positive perception and image of the government and its policies, both within the government ecosystem, among citizens, and globally. Adheres to guidelines on implementation of strict anti-corruption measures, vigilant oversight, and a commitment to integrity in public service.

Label: Handling Establishment Matters
Description: Demonstrates excellence in handling establishment matters through understanding extent and applicability of relevant rules, regulations, policies, for various establishment matters like holding DPCs (Departmental Promotion Committee), promotions, pay – fixation, transfer, posting, deputation, resignation, retirement, pension papers, employee grievances etc. to mitigate any potential grievance/complaints rising due to dissatisfaction of employees/staff. Involves setting new benchmarks and incentivizing the exceptional performance of employees. Conducts timely assessment of current and future manpower needs, such as officers, supporting staff, and MTS, in the Ministry/Department. Manages employee grievances, providing prompt, clear, concise, and fact/rule-based responses to minimize administrative tribunal cases.
Area Description: Demonstrates expertise in managing various processes and procedures related to establishment and human resource management, including manpower planning for efficient and optimal staffing, as well as managing service and personnel matters such as holding DPCs, managing promotions, pay fixation, allowances, transfers, postings, deputations, resignations, retirement, pension papers, employee grievances, reservations for various cadres, grades, and groups of services, and preventing sexual harassment within the Department or Ministry. Recognizes the accountability to provide prompt, accurate, and concise responses to employee queries by referring comprehensively to various FRSR, related rules, acts, policies, executive orders, etc., to satisfy government employees and mitigate factors that could lead to grievances or administrative tribunal cases.

Label: Policy Implementation
Description: Establishes and implements appropriate procedures to facilitate the implementation of central policies within the context of good governance, such as government programmes, schemes, missions, initiatives, etc.
Area Description: Demonstrates the capacity to detect gaps and improvement needs in existing policies in order to translate policy concepts into policy formulation for addressing existing and emerging needs of the citizens. Conducts thorough study and analysis of best practices at State, National, or Global levels, takes use of modern innovation and the most recent advancements in the social, economic, technological, and legal spheres while creating & implementing policies. Focuses on monitoring the application of policy and evaluating its impact and efficacy using a variety of instruments for responsible, open, and sustainable governance.

Label: RTI Responsiveness
Description: Understands the request/query/complaint/appeal and drafts responses by retrieving relevant information available in Section/Division and ensures timely disposal of the requests through PIO (Public Information Officer).
Area Description: Demonstrates thorough knowledge of RTI Act, 2005 and promptly & appropriately handles RTI responses. Drives proper maintenance of RTI records to ensure procedural compliance as per the established rules. Provides assistance to person(s) seeking information displaying transparency, good governance, and accountability.

Label: Procurement of Services/Goods/Works
Description: Demonstrates practical knowledge in regulatory and procedural compliance, procurement planning and execution and vendor management and risk management, by following the Rules & Regulations, Policies & Principles, and procedures in accordance with the "Manual on Procurement of Goods 2017" "Manual for Procurement of Services, 2022", General Financial Rules (GFR) 2017 and its amendments, and the DFPR (Indents, Contracts and Purchases for public service) guidelines.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to procure goods, services, or works using GeM (Government eMarketplace, National Public Procurement Portal) and other electronic portals, directly or through empanelled agencies, whether by way of purchase, lease, licence, or another method based on fundamental principles of transparency, fairness, competition, economy, efficiency, and accountability with respect to procurement. Ensures judicious use of public funds or any other source of funds (such as grants, loans, donations, or private investment), in accordance with GFR 2017, DFPR, 1978 (DFPR Rule 21, 21(a), 21(b), Manual of Procurement 2022 and their amendments, AOB, TOB, ToR.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Understands public procurement rules (e.g., GFR 2017, Manual for Procurement) and applies them to routine purchase cases, while using digital platforms like Government E-Marketplace (GeM) or Central Public Procurement Portal (CPPP) to initiate transactions and maintain documentation. Recognises fundamental procurement principles such as fair pricing, transparency, competition, and non-discrimination, applying them consistently to routine procurement cases. Understands requirements for secure record-keeping, approval workflows, vendor data confidentiality, and adherence to audit protocols in public procurement. Maintains purchase records and generates basic reports from e-procurement portals to track timelines, expenditures, and vendor responses. Recognises problems in basic procurement scenarios such as single bids, vague specifications, etc., and flags them to supervising authorities for escalation. Collaborates with procurement or finance officials for documentation, approvals, and vendor communication to complete end-to-end transactions.
Levels.Output_Description: I understand and apply basic public procurement rules and digital tools to make compliant and efficient purchases.  I am aware of the principles and objectives of public procurement, such as fairness, transparency, and value for money. I follow standard ethical guidelines and prescribed procedures in procurement to support fairness, security, and compliance.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Designs and executes complete procurement workflows such as bid preparation, vendor evaluations, and purchase finalisation, using digital portals (e.g. CPPP, GeM), aligned with regulatory and Departmental mandates. Evaluates vendor performance using procurement data, supplier records, past performance reports, and compliance checklists to optimise decision-making, pricing, and service delivery outcomes. Applies risk mitigation, audit-readiness, and compliance protocols to support cross-departmental coordination for efficient procurement aligned with M/D/O objectives. Drafts clear and complete tender documents, including scope of work, KPIs, and evaluation matrices, aligning with legal and ethical standards. Implements monitoring and reporting mechanisms to track vendor adherence to timelines, cost, and quality, ensuring alignment with contract requirements.  Reviews and validates procurement documents and ensures procurement plans are consistent with program timelines and budget forecasts.
Levels.Output_Description: I use procurement data, vendor records, and performance reports to assess vendor performance, optimise contracts, and deliver timely, value-based procurements. I apply compliance, risk, and inclusion frameworks across procurement stages to uphold legal and citizen service outcomes. I manage procurement processes to streamline planning and execution in alignment with organisational goals, enabling informed decisions that improve vendor performance, optimise pricing, and ensure timely delivery.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Designs procurement strategies using price trend analysis, risk assessment matrices, inventory planning tools, and regulatory guidelines to strengthen M/D/O planning and decision-making. Institutionalises ethical standards such as fairness, transparency, non-discrimination, and accountability in vendor engagement,  procurement platforms, ensuring alignment with GFR 2017, DFPR 1978, and their amendments, AOB, TOB, ToR. Leads procurement innovation (e.g., open framework agreements, startup sourcing) to drive transparency, responsiveness, and inclusion in public service delivery. Drives capacity building across teams on topics such as regulatory compliance, ethical contracting, sustainability standards, risk mitigation, procurement planning, vendor management, and effective use of e-procurement platforms like GeM and CPPP to ensure transparent and accountable procurement practices. Develops and uses reporting and analytics tools to compare procurement performance across departments, identify cost-saving opportunities, and improve overall efficiency. Advises leadership on emerging procurement risks and opportunities, including evolving regulatory requirements and innovative contracting practices. Promotes inter-departmental collaboration and standardises contracting practices to improve accountability and citizen engagement in public procurement.
Levels.Output_Description: I lead innovations in procurement by implementing advanced planning tools and strategies that strengthen compliance, increase transparency, and improve the delivery of goods and services.  I guide the adoption of ethical standards in procurement and ensure alignment with regulatory frameworks, embedding compliance and accountability into procurement systems and processes.  I institutionalise strategic procurement reforms to advance citizen centricity, competition, and public sector innovation.

Label: Data Use and Governance
Description: Utilises data based on prescribed rules, processes, and role delegations. Drives implementation of rules, policies, and procedures around data management to ensure its accuracy, consistency, security, and legality through a focus on data protection, data privacy, data quality management, and regulatory compliance.
Area Description: Collects, compiles, and assesses data, information, and knowledge resources in appropriate formats to enable interpretation and analysis for evidence-based decision making. Scrutinises data and reports with a keen eye for detail, evaluates their accuracy, relevance, trends, and anomalies, extracts insights, and contextualizes and critically analyses key points to develop data-driven strategic proposals and decisions while being mindful of prescribed rules & processes of data-handling within the government.

Label: Cabinet Note Writing
Description: Ensures adherence to the procedures for preparing and submitting papers to be considered by the Cabinet, Cabinet Committees, and Committee of Secretaries (CoS) Notes, including the use of the prescribed format, inclusion of all necessary elements, and appropriate presentation of the Cabinet Note. Capable of grading the Notes and taking into account any special circumstances while being aware of common deficiencies.
Area Description: Demonstrates comprehension of the significance of Cabinet Notes in policymaking, where the Cabinet and its committees make vital decisions that are crucial for efficient governance. This includes the ability to prepare Cabinet Notes that address various national and international matters affecting different areas of governance and present them to the Cabinet for consideration and decision-making. Therefore, a deep understanding of the AoB Rules, 1961 & ToB Rules, 1961 is essential, along with exceptional skills in preparing comprehensive Cabinet Notes that provide pertinent details and the right perspective.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Understands the structure, purpose, and process for preparing Cabinet and Committee of Secretaries (CoS) Notes as outlined in sections such as consolidated instructions, forwarding procedures, checklists for avoiding common deficiencies, types of cases, content requirements, and implementation protocols in the guidelines on Preparation of CoS Notes and the Handbook on Cabinet Notes while recognising their significance in policymaking. Understands applicable provisions (e.g., distribution of subjects, allocation of departments, inter-departmental consultations, submission protocols) of the Allocation of Business Rules, 1961 and Transaction of Business Rules, 1961 to appreciate their significance in policymaking and ensure procedural compliance. Supports the creation of Cabinet and CoS Notes by aligning content with national priorities such as Viksit Bharat@2047, Aatmanirbhar Bharat, Make in India, etc, through the use of updated national strategy documents and guidelines during the drafting process. Compiles papers, relevant data, annexures, and inter-ministerial inputs for inclusion in the  Cabinet or CoS Note to ensure that the Note is comprehensive. Identifies areas where procedural non-compliance occurs during documentation and seeks clarification or guidance to address them. Uses standard templates and checklists from the Handbook on Cabinet Notes to ensure inclusion of all required elements and logical sequencing, to support the effective preparation of Cabinet Notes. Uses digital tools (eOffice, eFile system, Microsoft Office, Single File System (SFS), Centralised Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS), Records Management System (RMS), File Tracking System (FMS), etc) for documentation and file management in line with Central Secretariat Manual of Office Procedure (CSMOP) guidelines. Maintains accurate and updated records, including version control, to support traceability, accountability, and smooth workflow during note preparation.
Levels.Output_Description: I understand the structure, purpose, and procedures for drafting Cabinet and CoS Notes to support accurate and well-structured note preparation. I draft Cabinet and CoS Notes using prescribed formats, integrating background material, precedents, inter-ministerial inputs, and annexures to ensure procedural compliance and completeness. I use official digital tools (e.g., eOffice, eFile, SFS, CPGRAMS, RMS, FMS, among others) to draft, format, manage versions, to ensure timely processing of Cabinet Notes in line with CSMOP and Cabinet Secretariat procedures.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Reviews drafts for completeness, clarity, coherence, and alignment with official guidelines (e.g. Preparation of CoS Notes, Handbook on Cabinet Notes), while checking for common deficiencies to improve quality. Assesses possible implications (such as legal, financial, institutional, or operational issues) and includes measures like clarifying ambiguities, ensuring legal soundness, or adding missing data to reduce risks and strengthen policymaking in Cabinet Notes. Identifies missing approvals and procedural gaps, as per the Allocation of Business (AoB) Rules, 1961 and the Transaction of Business Rules (ToB) Rules, 1961. Ensures that Cabinet and CoS Notes reflect alignment with national priorities such as Viksit Bharat@2047, Aatmanirbhar Bharat, Make in India, etc by linking proposals to relevant policy frameworks and strategic objectives, to support coherent, forward-looking decisions across levels of government. Integrates inputs and comments from all relevant line ministries, departments and organisations to ensure alignment, clarity, and completeness of the Cabinet or CoS Note. Reviews past Cabinet and CoS decisions, applicable rules, and precedents to maintain policy continuity and avoid inconsistencies. Coordinates timely routing, approvals, and formatting compliance of Cabinet and CoS Notes before final submission by using digital tools (e.g., eOffice, eFile, SFS, CPGRAMS, RMS, FMS, etc) in accordance with CSMOP guidelines. Guides officials on the use of prescribed formats, required elements, and presentation standards for preparing and submitting Cabinet, Cabinet Committee, and CoS Notes to ensure procedural compliance and build internal capacity.
Levels.Output_Description: I review Cabinet and CoS Notes for completeness, clarity, and alignment with the Handbook on Cabinet Notes and CoS Note Preparation Guidelines, and ensure they reflect national priorities to support decisions across levels  of government. I integrate inputs from multiple M/D/Os, flag procedural gaps, ensure alignment with AoB and ToB Rules, and use digital tools to support timely routing and approvals. I mentor officials on improving Cabinet and CoS Notes by refining structure, addressing common deficiencies, to ensure coherence in presenting recommendations.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Analyses the impact of Cabinet and CoS proposals on key stakeholders (e.g., Union, State, and local governments, citizens, voluntary organisations, private sector partners, etc.) to identify risks and implementation challenges, ensuring actionable decisions that lead to better policymaking. Reviews Cabinet and CoS Notes to ensure inter-ministerial alignment and clear explanation of legal, financial, operational, and public interest implications, and to justify any urgency provisions or exceptions beyond what is outlined in official guidelines. Designs internal quality assurance systems to grade, review, and provide feedback on cabinet and CoS notes across the department to improve consistency, procedural compliance, and the overall quality of notes that lead to better policymaking. Promotes the integration of digital tools (e.g. real-time dashboards, data platforms, eFile systems) and emerging technologies (e.g. AI, blockchain, etc) into official documentation practices such as Cabinet and CoS Notes, to support data-driven and citizen-centric policy-making. Engages with relevant departments to institutionalise digital tools (e.g. real-time dashboards, data platforms, eFile systems) and emerging technologies (e.g. AI, blockchain, among others) through updates to workflows, SOPs, and official manuals such as the  Preparation of CoS Notes, Handbook on Cabinet Notes and the CSMOP guidelines, among others. Represents the M/D/O  in strategic discussions with the Cabinet Secretariat, Cabinet meetings, Committee of Secretaries (CoS), Prime Minister’s Office (PMO), Office of the President, or other forums when required to explain, clarify, and defend the content, rationale, and implications of Cabinet and CoS Notes under consideration,  ensuring data-driven and citizen-centric policy-making.
Levels.Output_Description: I guide the development of Cabinet and CoS Notes and present a holistic analysis, risk considerations, and implementation steps  ensuring legal, financial, operational, and stakeholder impacts are addressed to support informed decision making. I design internal quality systems for Cabinet and CoS Notes and advocate embedding digital transformation, citizen-centric outcomes, and emerging data practices in CoS Notes, the Cabinet Notes Handbook, and CSMOP guidelines I represent the M/D/O  in strategic forums to explain and justify exceptions, urgency provisions, or special submissions, ensuring transparency and procedural rigour in decision-making.

Label: Procurement Management through GeM
Description: Demonstrates practical knowledge and ability in leveraging GeM (Government eMarketplace) as per Rule 149 of GFR, 2017, for procurement of common use goods and services and uses its tools of e-bidding, reverse e-auction, and demand aggregation, Guideline on Government eMarketplace (GeM) for all procurement needs of the Ministry/Department/Organisation.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to procure goods, services, or works using GeM (Government eMarketplace, National Public Procurement Portal) and other electronic portals, directly or through empanelled agencies, whether by way of purchase, lease, licence, or another method based on fundamental principles of transparency, fairness, competition, economy, efficiency, and accountability with respect to procurement. Ensures judicious use of public funds or any other source of funds (such as grants, loans, donations, or private investment), in accordance with GFR 2017, DFPR 1978 (DFPR Rule 21, 21(a), 21(b), Manual of Procurement 2022 and their amendments, AOB, TOB, ToR)

Label: RTI Record Management
Description: Maintains departmental records of RTIs for effective and comprehensive public disclosure of information of the Department.
Area Description: Demonstrates thorough knowledge of the RTI Act, 2005, and promptly & appropriately handles RTI responses. Drives proper maintenance of RTI records to ensure procedural compliance as per the established rules. Provides assistance to person(s) seeking information, displaying transparency, good governance, and accountability.

Label: Data Analysis and Visualisation
Description: Represents data through the use of common graphics, such as charts, plots, infographics, and even animations for effective decision making through identification of patterns, trends, and correlations.
Area Description: Collects, compiles, and assesses data, information, and knowledge resources in appropriate formats to enable interpretation and analysis for evidence-based decision-making. Scrutinizes data and reports with a keen eye for detail, evaluates their accuracy, relevance, trends, and anomalies, extracts insights, and contextualizes and critically analyses key points to develop data-driven strategic proposals and decisions while being mindful of prescribed rules & processes of data-handling within the government.

Label: AI/ML Tools
Description: Applies AI/ML tools to enhance efficiency and effectiveness of government systems while ensuring citizen-centric service delivery. Evaluates data readiness, deploys context-appropriate AI/ML solutions, and mitigates risks, biases, and security threats by following ethical, legal, and organisational protocols. Drives organisational adoption and transformation by mentoring personnel, fostering innovation, and collaborating with ecosystem stakeholders to co-create and scale responsible AI/ML solutions.
Area Description: Understands, identifies, implements, and utilizes digital tools for accurate, speedy, and effective delivery for superior visualization and decision making at different levels of the organization. Leverages digital technologies and platforms to collaborate effectively and efficiently with multiple stakeholders.

Label: Handling Allowances & Reimbursement
Description: Demonstrates understanding and ability to apply the relevant rules, regulations and guidelines for processing of Allowances and Reimbursements related matters in the Ministry, Department or Organisation.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to handle various administrative processes and procedures relating to Allowances & Reimbursement, Leave and Travel related matters. miscellaneous matters (Car, Residence, Personal Staff etc.), Official Language duty and ensures implementation across the Department / Ministry.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Identifies types of allowances and reimbursements under 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) and their ceilings, slabs, and conditions from Department of Expenditure (DoE) Office Memorandums (OMs)  and M/D/O specific instructions.  Understands tax exemption rules under Income Tax Act/CBDT circulars (for e.g., Children Education Allowance (CEA) exemption limits, newspaper/briefcase treatment, transport allowance rules, etc) and applies them while marking claims.  Explains eligibility conditions such as dependent criteria, school recognition, CGHS vs CS(MA) applicability, and documentation validity before processing claims on e-Office.   Checks each claim against eligibility, ceilings, and required documents using a standard checklist and returns incomplete cases with a feedback note to improve first-time-right submissions.  Verifies rate applicability from the relevant OM/rate list and documents the recalculated amount to demonstrate due diligence.  Processes sanctions with the correct head of account, attaches sanction‑bill‑receipt trails, and posts payments through PFMS/e‑Bill, to maintain traceability.  Uses authorised channels where prescribed (for e.g., empanelled providers/CGHS rates, approved telephone plans, etc) and secures prior sanction where required, to prevent inadmissible payments.  Maintains a monthly exception list (for e.g., over ceiling claims, repeated deficiencies, late submissions, etc) and escalates to supervisors to trigger corrective action.   Stores personal data (for e.g., medical records, dependency proofs) with need‑to‑know access and removal of sensitive data for RTI responses to protect privacy.
Levels.Output_Description: I understand key allowance rules and verify eligibility, ceilings, and rates before processing claims.  I complete the sanction and payment trails accurately ensuring claims are compliant, documented, and traceable.  I maintain compliant, well documented files that support transparent claim processing.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Conducts monthly claims governance reviews and pre-audit reviews, using dashboards (for e.g., claims received and settled, rejection rates, cycle time, etc) and systematic pre-verification against entitlements and sanctions to ensure timely settlement and minimise recoveries.   Standardises SOPs and maker–checker controls in e‑Office/HRMS (for e.g., mandatory fields, document proofs, auto‑calculation sheets, etc) to improve consistency and reduce manual errors.  Coordinates with CGHS/empanelled providers for medical claims to ensure use of approved packages, rates, and receipt formats, to prevent overpayment.   Tracks budget utilisation and prepares quarterly forecasts, highlighting risks of excesses or shortfalls to keep expenditure within approved grants. Coordinates with DDO/payroll to align tax flags and exemptions (for e.g., CEA, uniform, newspaper where applicable, etc) and validates reflection in payslips or Form 16, to ensure correct taxation.  Handles edge cases (for e.g., time‑barred claims, relaxation/condonation proposals, partial admissibility, etc) with rule citations and speaking orders, to make decisions defensible. Conducts quarterly training sessions for staff on allowance rules and documentation standards, and evaluates improvements in claim quality and turnaround time to build capacity. Responds to internal audit and RTI with document trails and rule references within timely, to uphold transparency.
Levels.Output_Description: I improve the accuracy and efficiency of claims through data-driven reviews, audits, and standardised processes.  I ensure financial discipline by keeping claims and budgets rule-compliant. I strengthen organisational capability by resolving complex cases with clear rule interpretation and by training staff to reduce errors.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Issues an annual Allowances and Reimbursement Administration Plan with SLAs (for e.g., scrutiny, settlement, and audit targets, savings goals, etc) and reviews with leadership to align performance of processing teams against defined SLAs.  Embeds digital workflows in HRMS, e‑Office, or PFMS (for e.g., validation rules, entitlement masters, exception reports, digital signatures, etc), and publishes an exceptions dashboard, to prevent errors and misuse.    Establishes risk‑based internal audit and commissions third‑party reviews tracking findings to closure with owners and due dates, strengthening assurance.    Establishes data protection controls (for e.g., retention schedules, masking/redaction for RTI, access logs, etc) for medical/identity data, conducting annual privacy briefings, to reduce data risk.   Aligns policies with GFR, DoE, OMs, etc instructions, and CBDT guidance, issuing consolidated standing orders and model templates, to harmonise practice across units.   Publishes quarterly analytics on spend by category, savings from process changes, and SLA adherence, using insights to refine targets and resource allocation, driving evidence‑based improvements.  Builds section capability by mentoring processors and reviewers and evaluating improvements through SLA and audit metrics.
Levels.Output_Description: I manage system risks through targeted audits and exception monitoring that prevent errors and misuse. I build organisational capability by mentoring staff and harmonising practices across units.

Label: Handling Leave and Travel
Description: Demonstrates understanding and ability to apply the relevant rules, regulations and guidelines for processing of Leave and Travel related matters in the Ministry, Department or Organization.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to handle various administrative processes and procedures relating to Allowances & Reimbursement, Leave and Travel related matters. miscellaneous matters (Car, Residence, Personal Staff etc.), Official Language duty and ensures implementation across the Department / Ministry.

Label: Handling Miscellaneous Matters (Car, Residence, Personal Staff etc.)
Description: Demonstrates understanding and ability to apply diverse provisions, methodologies, and protocols for overseeing miscellaneous affairs including Telephone, Car, Residence, Personal Staff etc in the Ministry, Department or Organization.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to handle various administrative processes and procedures relating to Allowances & Reimbursement, Leave and Travel related matters. miscellaneous matters (Car, Residence, Personal Staff etc.), Official Language duty and ensures implementation across the Department / Ministry.

Label: Rule of Business (AoB/ToB)
Description: Demonstrates a high level of proficiency in conducting the official business of the Government of India, as specified in the first schedule of the Allocation of Business Rules (AoB), 1961, which define the Ministries, Departments, Secretariats, and offices where the government business is transacted and outline the distribution of subjects among various departments, as specified in the second schedule of the rules. Demonstrates comprehension of the Transaction of Business (ToB) Rules, 1961, which outline the procedures for handling the business of the Government of India, including consultation with other departments and submission of cases to the Prime Minister, Cabinet and its Committees, and the President.
Area Description: Demonstrates comprehension of the significance of Cabinet Notes in policymaking, where the Cabinet and its committees make vital decisions that are crucial for efficient governance. This includes the ability to prepare Cabinet Notes that address various national and international matters affecting different areas of governance and present them to the Cabinet for consideration and decision-making. Therefore, a deep understanding of the AoB Rules, 1961 & ToB Rules, 1961 is essential, along with exceptional skills in preparing comprehensive Cabinet Notes that provide pertinent details and the right perspective.

Label: Inventory Management
Description: Manages receipt, storage, issue, verification, and disposal of goods and materials in government offices as per approved financial and procurement rules. Ensures traceability, accountability, and safety of items through accurate registers, timely reconciliation, and adherence to General Financial Rules (GFR 2017), Delegation of Financial Powers Rules (DFPR 1978), Manual for Procurement of Goods (2017), and related departmental instructions.
Area Description: Demonstrates ability to maintain accurate records of goods and materials in the Ministry / Department / Organization. Ensures proper custody of delivered items, conducts physical verification of assets, and identifies surplus and obsolete materials. Determines appropriate categories and modes of disposal for goods, such as auction or e-auction, in compliance with GFR, DFPR 1978, and Manual for Procurement of Goods 2017, to minimize losses due to various reasons.

Label: Contract Management
Description: Oversee and execute contractual agreements, characterised by practical expertise in various facets of the contracting process. Has the ability to conduct contract scoping, formulate well-defined Terms & Conditions, consider attached commercial nuances and legal aspects, and implement practices for risk mitigation in pursuit of the Public Good. Leverages domain knowledge to evaluate technical proficiency, skillfully negotiate contracts, and select the most suitable vendor, guided by principles such as transparency, fairness, competition, economy, efficiency, and accountability.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to procure goods, services, or works using GeM (Government eMarketplace, National Public Procurement Portal) and other electronic portals, directly or through empanelled agencies, whether by way of purchase, lease, licence, or another method based on fundamental principles of transparency, fairness, competition, economy, efficiency, and accountability with respect to procurement. Ensures judicious use of public funds or any other source of funds (such as grants, loans, donations, or private investment), in accordance with GFR 2017, DFPR, 1978 (DFPR Rule 21, 21(a), 21(b), Manual of Procurement 2022 and their amendments, AOB, TOB, ToR

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Identifies key stages of the contract lifecycle (for e.g., scoping, drafting, award, execution, amendment, and closure) and follows prescribed procedures for each stage under supervision. Understands the foundational aspects of the contracting lifecycle, including contract scoping, formulation of Terms & Conditions, commercial and legal considerations, and basic risk mitigation practices (for e.g. milestone-based payments, cost-plus contracts, etc.), to support effective public procurement guided by principles of transparency, fairness, efficiency, and accountability. Prepares initial drafts of contract documents  (for e.g. contract letters, change orders, notices, among others, etc) using approved templates, ensuring relevant terms and timelines are included.  Identifies routine deviations or errors in contract drafts (for e.g. missing clauses, ambiguous wording, etc) and flags them for corrective action to reduce delays and risks. Supports contract management practices such as procurement through eProcurement platforms (for e.g., GeM-CPPP, PPP, Post-Award Contract Management Toolkit, etc), compiling vendor data, and assisting in evaluations to promote transparency, fairness, and value-for-money in pursuit of the Public Good. Maintains records and updates status logs of contracts, deliverables, communications and clarifications to ensure transparency and availability for audit or reporting purposes. Refers to regulatory frameworks such as GFR 2017, DFPR 2024, the Manual for Procurement of Goods 2024, and departmental SOPs to ensure prescribed risk mitigation clauses (for e.g. milestone-based payments, performance security, LD clauses) are correctly included in contract drafts and flags deviations for correction to ensure legal soundness and procedural compliance.
Levels.Output_Description: I draft and update contract documents using approved templates, ensuring required clauses, timelines, and payment terms are accurately captured. I use procurement platforms to support vendor data compilation, assist in evaluations, and document key communications to ensure transparency, accuracy, and informed procurement decisions. I  follow GFR, DFPR, procurement manuals, and SOPs to ensure compliant and timely contract processing.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Reviews contracts and supporting documents such as RFPs, RFQs, award letters, and performance clauses, among others, to ensure alignment with GFR 2017, DFPR 2024, the Manual for Procurement of Goods 2024, departmental SOPs, and project-specific objectives, enabling outcome-based accountability and procedural compliance. Applies principles of transparency, competition, fairness, and accountability while documenting evaluation rationale, negotiation outcomes, and approvals at each stage of the contracting process. Prepares negotiation briefs by analysing cost benchmarks, risk allocation, payment structures, and vendor proposals, and records negotiated deviations through approved minutes and amendments. Conducts contract scoping by interpreting technical specifications, scope of work, and contextual requirements (for e.g., operating conditions, target beneficiaries, and expected service levels, etc) by applying domain knowledge to ensure relevance, feasibility, and value-for-money in pursuit of the Public Good. Coordinates with procurement, legal, and technical divisions to integrate specifications, SLAs, and dispute resolution clauses, supporting risk mitigation through mechanisms such as milestone-based payments, cost-plus contracts, among others.  Analyses vendor performance against contractual deliverables using dashboards or MIS tools and initiates corrective actions such as notices, penalty triggers, or extension proposals to ensure service continuity. Resolves disputes or misinterpretations in contract terms by referencing precedent cases and guidelines to prevent escalation. Uses contract monitoring dashboards (e.g. GeM-CPPP, PPP Post-Award Contract Management Toolkit, among others) and e-Procurement analytics (for e.g.,  bid comparisons, delivery timelines, etc) to track contract progress and support informed decision-making in pursuit of the Public Good. Mentors contract and procurement teams across M/D/Os  on good contracting practices and the use of procurement platforms.
Levels.Output_Description: I  review, finalise, and amend contracts to ensure legal compliance, clarity of obligations, and value-for-money outcomes. I coordinate with legal, technical, and procurement teams to manage risks, negotiations, and execution issues during the contract lifecycle. I monitor vendor performance and initiate timely corrective actions to ensure quality, timeliness, and accountability in service delivery.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Leads contract scoping and stakeholder consultations for high-value, multi-party, or inter-agency agreements (for e.g. centrally sponsored schemes, shared service arrangements, PPPs), by consolidating technical, legal, and financial inputs and assessing downstream execution risks, service continuity, and citizen impact. Guides and validates negotiation strategies for complex contracts by reviewing cost structures, risk allocation, payment mechanisms, and performance incentives, ensuring negotiated outcomes remain compliant with GFR provisions and principles of fairness and competition. Evaluates and resolves escalated contractual disputes, claims, extensions, and amendments by interpreting contract clauses, procurement rules, audit observations, and precedents, and records reasoned decisions to safeguard public interest and minimise litigation risk. Advocates for the institutionalisation of best practices in contract management, such as performance-linked contracts, risk-sharing mechanisms (for e.g. milestone-based payments, cost-plus contracts, etc.), and the use of emerging technologies (for e.g. blockchain, generative AI, etc.), to improve efficiency, transparency and traceability across M/D/Os. Reviews contract performance trends, audit findings, and dispute patterns to recommend targeted improvements in scoping, T&Cs, and risk mitigation approaches for future procurements, strengthening institutional learning and execution quality.
Levels.Output_Description: I lead and approve complex contract scoping and negotiation outcomes for high-value or multi-party procurements, ensuring compliance, fairness, and value-for-money.  I evaluate and resolve escalated contractual disputes to safeguard public interest and uphold transparency and accountability.  I strengthen contract execution quality by standardising clauses, risk mitigation approaches, and post-award monitoring practices based on audit findings and performance trends.

Label: Implementing Official Language
Description: Carries out all official communication within central and state government offices in accordance with the Official Languages Act, 1963, in Hindi. Promotes the usage of the Official Language in the day-to-day work in the Ministry, Department or Organization.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to handle various administrative processes and procedures relating to Allowances & Reimbursement, Leave and Travel related matters. miscellaneous matters (Car, Residence, Personal Staff etc.), Official Language duty and ensures implementation across the Department / Ministry.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Demonstrates foundational awareness of the Official Languages Act, 1963 and Official Languages Rules, 1976 (for e.g., including Region A/B/C classifications, Rules 5–8 on language of correspondence, etc) and identifies the office’s region accordingly. Prepares a simple one-page language-use map for inward/outward communications based on Rules 5–8 and circulates it within the section to guide correct language choice. Issues Section 3(3) category documents using approved bilingual templates and verifies that both versions meet OL requirements.  Drafts routine letters, forms, and notes in bilingual format, using Unicode Hindi and verifies correct rendering in e-Office, email, PDF, etc, to preserve readability.   Uses CSTT/Department of Official Language glossaries to apply standardised terminology in Hindi drafts.  Routes translation requests to the Hindi Section/empanelled translators with a clear purpose, target audience, and due date to obtain timely and fit-for-purpose translations.   Ensures bilingual visibility items (for e.g., nameplates, seals, signboards, website headers, etc) follow prescribed order and size, to ensure visible compliance.    Compiles Quarterly Progress Report (QPR) inputs (for e.g., Hindi noting/drafting percentage, 3(3) compliance, region-wise correspondence, etc) from section records to support accurate OLPRMS submission.  Prepares bilingual public-facing items such as RTI acknowledgements, CPGRAMS responses, notices, and forms following GIGW compliant formats.
Levels.Output_Description: I issue bilingual documents correctly by category and region, and I keep an auditable 3(3) register.  I use Unicode Hindi with approved terminology ensuring drafts are displayed correctly and read consistently.  I provide timely QPR data and run simple activities that increase everyday Hindi usage.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Convenes OLIC meetings with an agenda on QPR trends, 3(3) compliance, and region-wise correspondence and records minutes within timelines.   Conducts monthly sample audits of Section 3(3) documents and outward correspondence, issuing non-compliance notices using standard templates to drive compliance.   Generates MIS dashboards from e-Office (for e.g., Hindi noting percentage, bilingual outward percentage, 3(3) coverage, etc) and reviews trends with unit heads to understand areas of focus improvement.  Nominates officials to the Hindi Teaching Scheme (for e.g., typing, stenography, noting, drafting, etc) to build internal capacity where gaps exist.  Establishes translation workflows with style guides, glossaries, and peer review for critical documents to ensure consistency and reduce rework.  Reviews bilingual quality of website pages, forms, IVR/SMS templates, and brochures for GIGW compliance before release to ensure inclusive access.  Aligns procurement templates (e.g., NIT, bid, contract) with 3(3) requirements and verifies sample deliverables during pre-award checks to extend compliance to vendors.  Conducts root-cause analysis of recurring OL non-compliance and recommends corrective actions to the unit.  Plans, budgets, and administers contracts for translation, signage, training, and supporting technologies to institutionalise the use of the Official Language in official work, ensuring quality, compliance, and timeliness under the Official Languages Act, 1963.  Represents the office in TOLIC/Regional OLIC meetings, shares unit best practices, and adopts relevant directives, to harmonise implementation across organisations.  Coordinates with State Rajbhasha departments on inter-government communications to ensure alignment with official language requirements.
Levels.Output_Description: I run the OLIC cadence, publish usage dashboards, and close 3(3) non‑compliances within set timelines.  I coordinate training, translation, and bilingual updates across files, websites, and procurement.  I submit complete QPRs through OLPRMS and align our practice with TOLIC guidance.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Issues an annual Official Language Implementation Plan aligned to the DOL Annual Programme with wing-wise targets and reviews quarterly, to set direction and track outcomes.  Conducts internal inspections ahead of DOL inspections using a standard checklist (for e.g., registers, signage, training, dashboards, etc) to ensure readiness and continuous improvement.  Integrates Online Controls into e‑Office/PMIS (for e.g., mandatory bilingual templates, validation prompts, metadata for 3(3)), etc) to automate compliance.  Extends bilingual practice to citizen‑facing channels (for e.g., service portals, call centre scripts, notifications, etc) with user testing in Hindi and English to improve service experience.  Tracks regional reorganisation notifications and updated region-wise applicability of official language implementation rules to ensure compliance.    Publishes an annual bilingual compliance statement with metrics, audits, awards/recognitions, and improvement plans to build transparency and trust.  Nominates eligible units for Rajbhasha awards and mentors official language implementation leads to reinforce good practices and execution quality.
