Runs communities of practice to share solutions and lessons across units, tracking adoption of proven practices to enable scale and consistency.
Levels.Output_Description: I set direction and accountability for official language implementation through clear plans, targets, and regular reviews.  I institutionalise compliance by embedding controls into systems and daily processes.  I scale good practices by strengthening capability, recognising performance, and improving bilingual service delivery.

Label: File / Dak Management
Description: Demonstrates practical knowledge and ability in receiving, recording, organising, and monitoring official correspondence and files (physical and electronic) by defining classification, numbering, indexing, retention, and disposal frameworks, and ensures compliance with statutory, institutional, and security protocols. Applies tools and methodologies to track pendency, prioritise urgent dak, reconcile manual and digital registers, and leverage technology for real-time monitoring, audit readiness, and to ensure traceability, accountability, and timely decision-making across the file lifecycle.
Area Description: Administration and enforcement of compliance in accordance with the most recent "Central Secretariat Manual of Office Procedures" (CSMOP) to ensure that the Section, Division, or Department operates effectively and efficiently. Understands the issues and analyses precedents, facts, ruling position, etc., and records remarks and proposes actions for enabling the competent authority to review and make decisions for timely disposal. Concisely drafts written correspondence such as O.M., memos, notices, reports, letters, e-mails, etc., to be issued on approval of the competent authority. Articulates all kinds of communications towards administration, policy administration, implementation, advisory, reporting, proposal writing and any other documentation towards handling departmental matters with a focus on good governance.

Label: PEST (Political, Economic, Social, Technological) Consciousness
Description: Maintains awareness of the national political leadership's vision for the country, as well as its strategic communications and collaborations with various national and global entities revolving around the economic, social or technological domains, in relation to the Department/Ministry/Organisation.
Area Description: Ensures citizens’ welfare and satisfaction by improving the standards of accountable and transparent governance, implementing result-oriented administration through innovative interventions based on design thinking, and enhancing citizen-centric decision-making. Implements Open Government policies and practices, which include collaborating and forming partnerships with informed citizens, leveraging social capital, and promoting participative public service delivery by ensuring freedom and ease of access to information. Establish an efficient framework that ensures the timely and transparent delivery of services by various government departments through awareness of various social, political, economic and technological developments that may impact citizens.

Label: Expenditure Management
Description: Demonstrates the ability to adopt internal control mechanisms for efficient & effective financial operations as well as identify potential threats & opportunities for the Ministry / Department/ Organisation.
Area Description: Formulates budget and expenditure plans aligned to the objectives and budget allocation of the Ministry / Department / Organisation, in accordance with the rules and guidelines of the Ministry of Finance, while also forecasting future expenditures. Manages and monitors the Division/Department's budget, accounts & expenditure, while promoting financial prudence within the Department to enhance overall efficiency. Develops proficiency in handling PFMS (Public Financial Management System).

Label: Submission of briefs, supply of information
Description: Demonstrates the ability to prepare responses to parliamentary questions for the Minister through suitable data analysis, coordination, and articulation of responses to parliamentary questions.
Area Description: Ensures the smooth conduct of Parliamentary proceedings through preparation, maintenance, and management of systems and processes in the Division / Department related to handling parliamentary affairs. Anticipates key questions, supports in preparation of responses/briefs, and demonstrates agility in supplying information to key stakeholders.

Label: Proposal preparation for disciplinary proceedings
Description: Demonstrates prudence and clarity in comprehending and implementing The Public Servants Enquiries Act and action on complaints forwarded by the Administrative Ministry/Department. Disposal of complaints of non-vigilance nature, Action on anonymous/pseudonymous complaints, procedure for dealing with complaints and engagement of retired officials to conduct Investigation and perform other vigilance functions
Area Description: Demonstrates a thorough understanding and practical application of administrative vigilance and disciplinary procedures, which serve as safeguards to ensure the proper implementation of government processes, policies, and rules, while identifying instances of breach by government employees who may abuse their authority or power of approval for personal gain. Facilitates in building a positive perception and image of the government and its policies, both within the government ecosystem, among citizens, and globally. Adheres to guidelines on the implementation of strict anti-corruption measures, vigilant oversight, and a commitment to integrity in public service.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Demonstrates awareness of essential legal policies, rules, etc., e.g., the Public Servants Enquiries Act, CCS (Conduct, Discipline & Appeal) Rules (Chapter 18), Revised AIS Rule (Vol I, Rule 12) and guidelines from the Vigilance Manual (CVC 2021). Applies basic principles of natural justice, maintains confidentiality in routine tasks, and adheres to prescribed procedural timelines with consistency (for e.g., uses standard confidentiality covers, vigilance file classification codes, etc).  Identifies complaint category (for e.g., vigilance, non-vigilance, or A/P) using the CVC admissibility grid or departmental categorisation matrix. Comprehends administrative processes related to disposal of complaints, including non-vigilance cases, anonymous/pseudonymous complaints, and the role of retired officials in investigations. Updates vigilance case progress using departmental MIS fields such as ‘Stage’, ‘Timeline’, ‘Action Taken’, and ‘Pending Inputs’. Assists disciplinary authorities in the preparation of preliminary documentation, charge sheets, and related reports. Utilises prescribed formats and tools (for e.g., file management software, e-office systems, vigilance file index formats, etc) to maintain and organise case files. Observes ethical standards and confidentiality protocols during documentation and communication management.
Levels.Output_Description: I collect and document complaints and initial evidence to determine if disciplinary action is warranted. I identify relevant rules and route the case to the correct authority. I maintain secure, complete, and traceable case files in e-Office.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Applies comprehensive knowledge of relevant rules and departmental vigilance procedures to independently draft clear and legally coherent charge sheets and disciplinary proposals (for e.g., by using CCS(CCA) charge sheet templates, annexure formats, etc). Conducts detailed reviews of complaints from varied sources, including anonymous inputs, using CVC admissibility matrices, vigilance triage grids, etc and assesses admissibility. Coordinates collection and validation of evidence, facilitates engagement with retired officials for investigative support when needed (for e.g., using document-authentication formats, witness lists, and official requisition templates).  Ensures due process, adherence to fair inquiry principles, and compliance with timelines using statutory timeline charts, issuance logs, and vigilance MIS alert functions.  Maintains effective liaison with vigilance sections, legal departments, and administrative authorities for smooth procedural flow. Tracks case status using vigilance digital dashboards, pendency trackers, and workflow audit logs for transparency. Reviews regulatory updates using DoPT circulars and departmental regulatory trackers. Prepares draft FRs and speaking notes using approved FR-drafting templates and rule-citation reference sheets. Documents mandatory consultations (for e.g., vigilance, legal, CVC, UPSC, etc) using consultation registers, advice-upload modules, and advice-reference coding formats.
Levels.Output_Description: I draft clear, rule-aligned disciplinary proposals and charge sheets using prescribed formats. I ensure admissibility, due process, and compliance with statutory timelines.  I coordinate with relevant stakeholders to facilitate effective case management.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Leads process optimisation and reform initiatives to enhance the efficacy and compliance of disciplinary proceedings. Provides expert advice on legislative amendments, provisions and policy updates, including integration of amended CCS (Conduct, Discipline & Appeal)/AIS (All India Service) rules and the Vigilance Commission’s guidelines. Champions capacity building interventions to build team competencies in disciplinary preparation and case management. Institutionalises systems for expedited disposal of disciplinary matters (for e.g., using pendency dashboards, automated case aging trackers, etc), incorporating risk mitigation and ethical safeguards. Engages senior administrative and vigilance officials, fostering an environment free from undue influence or coercion. Drives adoption of advanced investigative and analytical tools, including AI-enabled tools, for e.g., fraud-risk scoring models, text-analysis utilities, and document-comparison algorithms, etc for evidence analysis and fraud detection. Cultivates organisational culture of transparency, accountability, and public trust through exemplary governance standards.
Levels.Output_Description: I lead transparent, timely, and rule-aligned disciplinary disposal processes. I mentor teams, guide policy advocacy, and foster adoption of innovative tools and ethical safeguards. I ensure impartial, timely, and transparent disposal of disciplinary cases, reinforcing institutional integrity.

Label: Evaluation of Outcomes/Outputs
Description: Conducts assessment based on the data to identify strengths and shortfalls in intended outcomes and formulates remedial actions to correct deficiencies in delivery of schemes and government programs.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to develop monitoring and evaluation framework for tracking implementation of central government policies, schemes, and projects for the Department/ Ministry/ Organization. Identifies right set of data, information, and relevant parameters to evaluate. Undertakes qualitative and quantitative assessments to determine the progress of a government policy, schemes and project towards pre-specified goals and objectives, and to highlight any alarming gap that may hamper the slated outcomes & outputs. Facilitates in making informed decisions and adjustments to improve policy effectiveness, ensuring policies lead to desired outcomes and benefits. Identify successes, challenges, and lessons learned to enhance future policymaking and implementation processes for the Government of India.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Identifies prescribed output and outcome indicators from scheme guidelines, OOMF documents, or DMEO templates, and understands what each indicator is intended to measure. Recognises DPDP and RTI requirements for scheme data and follows approved checklists and SOPs for masking PII, sharing aggregates, and maintaining basic access records. Maps the scheme logic (inputs - activities - outputs - outcomes) using standard templates to understand where performance should be assessed.  Defines indicators (mix of output and immediate outcome) with SMART metadata (for e.g., definition, unit, frequency, disaggregation, etc) in a data dictionary for supervisor approval.   Extracts and organises data from authorised sources (for e.g., scheme MIS, PFMS, dashboards, survey summaries, etc) as per defined formats and timelines.  Flags strengths, shortfalls, and data quality issues in summary notes or dashboards for supervisory review.  Compares actual values against baselines and targets (for e.g., using predefined templates, variance thresholds, or system-generated flags) to identify areas of underperformance or overachievement.   Uses basic disaggregation (for e.g., district, beneficiary group, time period, etc) to highlight visible gaps, as per predefined parameters.   Documents assumptions, data limitations, and missing information to avoid misinterpretation of results. Files all artefacts (for e.g., logic model, dictionary, datasets, dashboard, action log, etc) in e‑Office with version control, to keep an auditable trail.
Levels.Output_Description: I identify strengths and shortfalls in scheme outputs and outcomes using prescribed indicators and data sources.  I organise and present performance data clearly to support review and decision making.   I maintain accurate, auditable data records and flag data limitations transparently.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Designs an evaluation plan (for e.g., including questions, indicators, sampling, timeline, etc) for districts and states.   Collects primary data using stratified samples and standard tools (for e.g., FGDs, KIIs, structured surveys, etc), ensuring high response rates and inter‑rater reliability checks to improve validity.  Integrates administrative data (e.g., MIS, PFMS, etc) with primary data sources to create a consolidated dataset for assessment.  Applies standard analytical techniques (for e.g., trend or cohort analysis) on integrated datasets to assess changes in outputs and outcomes over time.  Documents data sources, assumptions, and analytical steps in a methods note to enable review and reproducibility of findings.  Assesses cost, efficiency, and coverage in relation to observed outcome shifts to support value-for-money analysis.  Builds disaggregated views (for e.g., gender, caste, geography, vulnerability groups, etc) and tests gaps, flags equity shortfalls with confidence intervals, to target inclusion.   Performs root cause analysis (for e.g., fishbone, 5‑Whys, etc) on underperforming indicators and proposes CAPA (for e.g., process, staffing, outreach, supply changes, etc) to enable action.  Presents assessment findings and recommended remedial actions to supervisors or steering committees for decision making.  Tracks implementation of agreed corrective actions and reports early signs of improvement or persistence of gaps. Publishes anonymised datasets and codebooks on approved platforms (for e.g., e‑Office link or data.gov.in, etc) with metadata, to support transparency and reuse.
Levels.Output_Description: I conduct structured assessments to explain why scheme outcomes and outputs are performing as observed.  I recommend data-backed remedial actions to address delivery and design gaps.  I track whether corrective actions lead to measurable improvements in performance.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Institutionalises evaluation frameworks, standards, and schedules across programmes to ensure consistent assessment of outcomes and outputs.  Sets an annual evaluation calendar (for e.g., in terms of process, outcome, impact studies, etc) with independent/third‑party components, allocating budget and publishes ToRs with quality assurance criteria, to ensure rigor.   Establishes portfolio analytics (for e.g., outcomes achieved vs targets, cost‑effectiveness, equity gaps, risk flags, etc) and uses them in quarterly reviews to recommend scale, stop, and/or reset of schemes, to optimise impact.  Links evaluation evidence to budget allocations, performance reviews, and policy amendments. Ensures accountability by publicly disclosing evaluation findings and tracking closure of agreed corrective actions on transparent dashboards (for e.g., service time saved, leakage reduced, coverage gaps closed, etc). Enforces privacy, ethics, and accessibility (for e.g., DPIA, consent/notice, anonymisation, GIGW conformance, etc) across all studies and artefacts, audits compliance annually, to reduce risk.  Coordinates with states, PSUs, line ministries, etc to harmonise indicators and share best practices.  Builds institutional evaluation capacity by mandating management responses and learning notes for major assessments and running communities of practice or certification mechanisms to strengthen evaluator skills and shared learning in the M/D/O. Leads M/D/O learning by commissioning meta-evaluations or learning syntheses and systematically updating indicator catalogues or playbooks based on consolidated lessons. Uses evaluation evidence to support policy and legal decisions by clearly documenting the rationale for actions taken.
Levels.Output_Description: I institutionalise assessment systems such that evidence consistently informs policy and funding decisions.  I ensure remedial actions lead to sustained improvements through programme redesign and system changes.  I use evaluation evidence to strengthen accountability and long-term public value across schemes programmes.

Label: Handling Fundamental Rules/Supplementary Rules
Description: Demonstrates understanding of the extent and application of Fundamental Rules and Supplementary Rules, including their definitions, relevance, and relation to other rules, guiding principles, general conditions of service, joining procedures, transfers, pay, allowances, delegations, pension, and associated O.M.s.
Area Description: Demonstrates expertise in managing various processes and procedures related to establishment and human resource management, including manpower planning for efficient and optimal staffing, as well as managing service and personnel matters such as holding DPCs, managing promotions, pay fixation, allowances, transfers, postings, deputations, resignations, retirement, pension papers, employee grievances, reservations for various cadres, grades, and groups of services, and preventing sexual harassment within the Department or Ministry. Recognises the accountability to provide prompt, accurate, and concise responses to employee queries by referring comprehensively to various FRSR, related rules, acts, policies, executive orders, etc., to satisfy government employees and mitigate factors that could lead to grievances or administrative tribunal cases.

Label: LIMBS Portal Management
Description: Leverages LIMBS portal for registering on the platform, working as a local officer or nodal admin, entering new cases and editing existing ones, keeping track of hearing dates, and generating reports from MIS reports, dashboard, and other related details to effectively manage and monitor court cases on the portal.
Area Description: Provides support during legal proceedings pertaining to the Union of India by demonstrating legal know-how and engaging law officers or approved panel counsels for a range of court cases. Keeps track of and manages information and updates regarding multiple cases on the LIMBS portal to ensure timely and necessary actions.

Label: Handling Prosecution Cases
Description: Demonstrates ability and understanding to support investigation processes aptly and promptly, handling procedures for prosecution cases, and imposing minor and major penalties.
Area Description: Demonstrates a thorough understanding and practical application of administrative vigilance and disciplinary procedures, which serve as safeguards to ensure the proper implementation of government processes, policies, and rules, while identifying instances of breach by government employees who may abuse their authority or power of approval for personal gain. Facilitates in building a positive perception and image of the government and its policies, both within the government ecosystem, among citizens, and globally. Adheres to guidelines on implementation of strict anti-corruption measures, vigilant oversight, and a commitment to integrity in public service.

Label: Handling Matters of Reservation
Description: Demonstrates the ability to administer and implement reservation policies in appointments, promotions, and service matters in accordance with government instructions. This includes preparing and maintaining accurate reservation rosters, applying prescribed relaxations or concessions in age, fees, and qualifications, and ensuring representation for Scheduled Castes (SCs), Scheduled Tribes (STs), Other Backward Classes (OBCs), Persons with Disabilities (PwDs), Ex-servicemen (including re-employment in central services and posts), and Economically Weaker Sections (EWSs). The competency also covers interpreting amendments, circulars, and judicial pronouncements related to reservations, updating procedures accordingly, and ensuring compliance across cadres, services, and posts within the Ministry/Department/Organization. It emphasizes fairness, transparency, and accountability in managing reservations, while addressing shortfalls, backlogs, and audit requirements through timely monitoring and reporting.
Area Description: Demonstrates expertise in managing various processes and procedures related to establishment and human resource management, including manpower planning for efficient and optimal staffing, as well as managing service and personnel matters such as holding DPCs, managing promotions, pay fixation, allowances, transfers, postings, deputations, resignations, retirement, pension papers, employee grievances, reservations for various cadres, grades, and groups of services, and preventing sexual harassment within the Department or Ministry. Recognizes the accountability to provide prompt, accurate, and concise responses to employee queries by referring comprehensively to various FRSR, related rules, acts, policies, executive orders, etc., to satisfy government employees and mitigate factors that could lead to grievances or administrative tribunal cases.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Identifies constitutional provisions and basic Government of India instructions governing reservations for SC, ST, OBC, PwD, EWS, and Ex-servicemen (for e.g., (e.g., Articles 16(4) and 16(4A) of the Constitution, EWS reservation guidelines, PwD reservation instructions under the RPwD Act, etc).   Understands the purpose and structure of post-based reservation rosters and recognises their relevance in recruitment and promotion cases.  Applies DoPT instructions (for e.g., post-based roster OMs, reservation instructions for recruitment/promotion,  backlog vacancy guidelines, etc) to routine appointment and promotion cases by following prescribed checklists and formats under guidance. Prepares and updates reservation rosters using approved formats (for e.g., manual or eHRMS/Excel-based) with attention to detail.  Records relaxations and concessions (for e.g., age, qualification, fees, etc) strictly as per notified rules (for e.g., e.g., DoPT age-relaxation instructions, PwD and EWS reservation guidelines, recruitment rules/examination notifications, etc) and maintains supporting documentation. Processes files through e-Office, ensuring correct noting, drafting, and placement of approvals related to reservation matters. Maintains systematic records to support RTI responses and audit scrutiny, escalating ambiguities or deviations for guidance. Demonstrates awareness of fairness, non-discrimination, and transparency as core principles underpinning reservation implementation.
Levels.Output_Description: I accurately maintain reservation rosters using prescribed formats and tools.  I process routine recruitment and promotion cases by correctly applying reservation rules under supervision.  I maintain complete and traceable documentation for concessions, relaxations, and approvals.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Validates reservation rosters across groups, cadres, and services using prescribed verification protocols (for e.g., DoPT post-based roster checks, cadre strength–roster reconciliation, pre-DPC roster validation, etc) to ensure correct representation.  Monitors appointments, promotions, and application of relaxations and concessions to ensure compliance with DoPT/Ministry of Finance guidelines across recruitment, promotion, and service-related cases.  Manages and validates reservation rosters across groups, cadres, and services to ensure proper representation across vertical and horizontal reservation categories (for e.g., SC, ST, OBC, EWS, PwD, Ex-servicemen, etc).  Interprets amendments, clarifications, and court directions related to reservation policies and applies them consistently to ongoing cases.  Coordinates with recruitment agencies, cadre controlling authorities, administrative divisions, and audit/vigilance units to ensure end-to-end compliance.  Analyses cadre-wise data to identify shortfalls, backlogs, and deviations (for e.g., shortfall in SC/ST/OBC/PwD representation against prescribed percentages, backlog vacancies due to unfilled reserved posts, deviations from post-based roster points or cadre strength, etc) and initiates corrective actions in consultation with stakeholders.  Prepares consolidated reports on reservation status, backlog vacancies, and representation trends using MIS or dashboards for senior officers and DPCs.  Responds to audit observations and RTI queries on reservation matters by providing accurate, rule-based, and well-documented replies.  Implements feedback from audits, grievances, and court cases to improve reservation processes and prevent recurrence of errors.
Levels.Output_Description: I ensure reservation rosters and HR actions comply with rules across cadres and services.  I apply government clarifications and judicial directions accurately to complex reservation cases.  I provide reliable reservation status and backlog reports to support informed decision making.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Designs and institutionalises frameworks for reservation governance, including standardised roster validation SOPs, cadre-level reservation review cycles, backlog and shortfall monitoring mechanisms, and escalation protocols for non-compliance or disputes. Evaluates M/D/O practices against constitutional provisions, DoPT instructions, and judicial pronouncements to ensure sustained compliance. Provides interpretation and resolution in complex, disputed, or litigation-prone reservation matters (for e.g., promotion related reservation disputes, backlog vacancy carry forward, seniority issues, or horizontal reservation application, etc). Anticipates legal, audit, and reputational risks arising from reservation implementation and ensures decisions are legally defensible and auditable. Oversees system-level monitoring of cadre representation, backlogs, and compliance through dashboards and periodic reviews. Strengthens transparency and trust by ensuring proactive disclosures, timely reporting, and consistent handling of reservation-related grievances. Advises leadership on strategic HR and manpower planning implications of reservation policies and representation trends.
Levels.Output_Description: I provide guidance on complex and disputed reservation matters.  I ensure M/D/O-wide compliance with reservation policies, court rulings, and audit requirements.  I strengthen equitable representation and institutional credibility through system-level oversight.

Label: Handling Performance Appraisal
Description: Demonstrates understanding of the Annual Performance Assessment Report process for each Government employee, enabling evaluation of performance and determining their suitability for career progression opportunities such as confirmation, promotion, selection for deputation, or selection for foreign assignments.
Area Description: Demonstrates expertise in managing various processes and procedures related to establishment and human resource management, including manpower planning for efficient and optimal staffing, as well as managing service and personnel matters such as holding DPCs, managing promotions, pay fixation, allowances, transfers, postings, deputations, resignations, retirement, pension papers, employee grievances, reservations for various cadres, grades, and groups of services, and preventing sexual harassment within the Department or Ministry. Recognises the accountability to provide prompt, accurate, and concise responses to employee queries by referring comprehensively to various FRSR, related rules, acts, policies, executive orders, etc., to satisfy government employees and mitigate factors that could lead to grievances or administrative tribunal cases.

Label: Design thinking
Description: Demonstrates ability to decode intertwined administrative systems, understand dynamic and multifaceted situations, based on a solid foundation of study of need & demand of target population. Approaches problems with an empathic, “beginner’s mind”, openness, & optimism for creative and human-centered solutions. Follows a systematic and conscious approach to public service delivery that involves being open and impartial to the perspectives, grievances, and recommendations of service users and stakeholders, especially those from diverse social groups.
Area Description: Ensures citizens’ welfare and citizens’ satisfaction, by improving the standards of accountable and transparent governance, implementing result-oriented administration through innovative interventions based on design thinking, and enhancing citizen-centric decision-making. Implements Open Government policies and practices, which include collaborating and forming partnerships with informed citizens, leveraging social capital, and promoting participative public service delivery by ensuring freedom and ease of access to information. Establish an efficient framework that ensures timely and transparent delivery of services by various government departments through awareness of various social, political, economic and technological developments that may impact citizens.

Label: Handling Matters of Prevention of Sexual Harassment (PoSH) Policy
Area Description: Demonstrates awareness, sensitivity, and responsibility in preventing and addressing sexual harassment at the workplace. Applies the legal framework and organisational procedures under the PoSH Act, 2013, to ensure a safe, respectful, and inclusive work environment, while upholding principles of fairness, confidentiality, neutrality, and accountability.

Label: Change Readiness
Description: Communicates effectively, enabling to comprehend the cause and purpose of proposed change, the vision of the change, and the anticipated impact (such as increased efficiency in processes, transparency, speed, accuracy, etc.) for the beneficiaries (including government employees, staff, citizens, etc.), in order to foster willingness and preparedness to adopt the change.
Area Description: Adopts a structured approach for the effective initiation and implementation of multi-level, multi-faceted changes in the government ecosystem, with the aim of achieving a prescribed mandate as part of a Govt. program, scheme, project, or government initiative. Drives preparedness to unlearn and relearn, communicates the need for change to gain stakeholder acceptance and shared commitment, and drives change from the current state to the desired future state.

Label: Change Implementation
Description: Demonstrates the ability to plan, execute, monitor, and institutionalise approved changes by translating policy, programme, or organisational decisions into sustained operational practices that achieve intended outcomes.
Area Description: Adopts a structured approach for the effective initiation and implementation of multi-level, multi-faceted changes in the government ecosystem, with the aim of achieving a prescribed mandate as part of a Govt. program, scheme, project, or government initiative. Drives preparedness to unlearn and relearn, communicates the need for change to gain stakeholder acceptance and shared commitment, and drives change from the current state to the desired future state.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Understands the purpose, scope, and intended outcomes of the approved change by reviewing official instructions and briefings, so that assigned tasks are carried out correctly and in alignment with the change objective.  Identifies the specific change-related tasks required within their role or unit by referring to the implementation plan and role guidance (for e.g., Responsibility Matrix, Section-wise Task Allocation Sheet, e-Office note assigning duties, etc) ensuring clarity on responsibilities and reducing execution errors.  Assesses whether basic requirements for implementation, including staff availability and clarity of instructions, are in place to avoid delays or incomplete implementation.  Executes assigned change activities in the prescribed sequence and within agreed timelines to ensure consistency and compliance with the implementation plan.  Communicates approved implementation instructions using prescribed circulars, meeting notes, or digital channels, so that affected stakeholders receive clear and reliable information.   Maintains basic records of actions taken, instructions received, and immediate outcomes in the prescribed format, and systems such as e-Office, file notes, MIS Updates, etc, supporting traceability and accountability during implementation.
Levels.Output_Description: I understand the purpose of approved changes and what is expected during implementation.  I carry out assigned change activities as per approved instructions, timelines, and methods.  I track progress and flag issues early to support effective implementation.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Assesses team readiness by examining capacity, coordination, and operational constraints to support effective adoption of the approved change (for e.g, checking staff deployment review, workload sheet analysis, fund availability check through PFMS, system readiness checklist, etc). Aligns available human and financial resources with implementation processes and timelines (for e.g., revising expenditure plan in PFMS, reallocating staff through office order, updating Gantt chart, etc) to ensure priorities are realistically resourced and executable. Implements approved changes to procedures, systems, and role responsibilities to embed the change into routine operations. Coordinates approved communication interventions for affected stakeholders to ensure an accurate understanding of the change. Coordinates approved capacity building interventions to enable stakeholders to implement the change in their routine functions. Uses defined indicators to track adoption, performance, and early outcomes, and reviews them regularly to assess whether the change is being implemented as intended. Reviews implementation data and feedback to identify delays, uneven adoption, or emerging risks, enabling timely corrective action. Initiates or suggests corrective actions such as adjusting timelines, clarifying roles, or strengthening support to address gaps in execution or adoption. Documents and consolidates implementation learnings to inform subsequent phases or future initiatives, strengthening M/D/O learning.
Levels.Output_Description: I implement approved changes to procedures, systems, or role responsibilities to support routine operational use. I use defined implementation indicators to monitor adoption and take corrective action where required. I document implementation learnings to support subsequent phases of rollout.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Establishes M/D/O-wide standards, roles, and governance mechanisms for change implementation by defining clear accountabilities and decision rights, ensuring consistency and reliability across initiatives. Ensures implementation readiness inputs inform policy approval and rollout decisions, improving feasibility and delivery outcomes. Ensures readiness and implementation risk assessments are reviewed prior to scale-up or phased rollout, preventing overextension and implementation failure. Embeds adoption and outcome indicators into M/D/O performance and review systems (for e.g., monthly dashboard review, inclusion in Departmental Performance Review, PRAGATI-style monitoring format, etc) enabling leadership oversight of implementation quality and results. Establishes structured internal or independent review mechanisms to assess implementation quality and outcomes, strengthening accountability and learning. Reviews the cumulative impact of multiple concurrent change initiatives on organisational capacity and performance, enabling prioritisation and sequencing decisions. Codifies effective change implementation practices into institutional guidance, toolkits, and reference materials, enabling repeatability and scale. Ensures new processes and practices are embedded into routine organisational systems and reviews, sustaining change outcomes beyond initial implementation.
Levels.Output_Description: I establish M/D/O-wide standards and ownership for implementing approved changes. I use routine M/D/O performance and review systems to assure implementation quality and adoption. I ensure that implemented changes are embedded into standard administrative processes and reviews.

Label: Research and Need Analysis
Description: Analyses relevant reports, surveys, papers, etc. to identify the needs, the beneficiaries, returns or strategic benefits to the citizens and the Nation at large (both in tangible and intangible terms).
Area Description: Demonstrates knowledge and ability to identify, define, plan, develop, deliver & monitor government programs, schemes to realise the agreed benefits for the citizens within the prescribed timeframe. Oversees and coordinates a group of related projects within a government program to achieve specific benefits and maintain control. Demonstrates the ability in Program Selection, Program Planning, Program/ Project Transitions, Program Implementation, Strategic Program Management, Program Progress Monitoring and Review, Program Analysis, Program Financing, Program/ Project Change Management, Benefits Management, Stakeholder Relationships and Collaboration.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Gathers data from internal and external sources (for e.g., census reports, NSSO surveys, previous program evaluations, RBI bulletins, Ministry annual reports, etc) to understand the program context and citizen need. Organises data, reports, surveys, papers using standard frameworks (for e.g., PESTEL, RACI, etc) to enable structured analysis of citizen needs, beneficiaries, and potential program outcomes. Identifies target beneficiaries and their key needs by analysing data from sources such as surveys, evaluations, or government reports (for e.g., census, NSSO, RBI bulletins, etc) to ensure that program design is evidence-based and citizen-focused. Assesses expected tangible (for e.g., enrolment rates, income growth, etc) and intangible outcomes (for e.g., improved trust, safety, wellbeing) to understand how proposed programs will contribute to citizen welfare and national priorities. Assesses trends and patterns in qualitative and quantitative data, categorising data and information, identifying gaps, conducting correlation analysis, etc, to support evidence-based conclusions. Documents findings using standard tools (for e.g., eOffice, MS Excel, dashboards, prescribed reporting templates, etc) ensuring accuracy and traceability. Summarises research findings and insights clearly for stakeholders (for e.g., citizens, civil society groups, private firms, political leadership, etc) in prescribed formats to support evidence-based decision-making.
Levels.Output_Description: I collect relevant data and information from internal and external sources to understand program context and citizen needs. I analyse data using standard frameworks to identify beneficiaries, key needs, and potential program outcomes. I prepare concise research briefs that support evidence-based program decisions.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Integrates qualitative and quantitative data from multiple sources (for e.g., surveys, MIS dashboards, PFMS expenditure trends, evaluation studies, etc) to identify implementation gaps and emerging needs, informing program design.  Evaluates reliability, limitations, and assumptions of datasets (for e.g., reviews survey methodology and sample size, checks recency and completeness of MIS data, cross-verifies PFMS figures with utilisation reports, etc) before drawing conclusions. Applies analytical frameworks such as cost-benefit analysis, SROI, LogFrame, MCDA, etc, to articulate both tangible and intangible benefits to citizens and the nation. Builds team capacity in using structured analytical tools (for e.g., Cost-Benefit Analysis, LogFrame, MIS analysis, etc) to strengthen evidence-based program design. Uses data analytics and visualisation tools (for e.g., Power BI, Tableau, Microsoft Office, etc) to generate evidence and ensures their effective adoption by team members to support data-driven decision-making for new programs or schemes. Coordinates with internal and external stakeholders to expand information sources, validate data, and ground-truth findings, ensuring research reflects operational realities. Synthesises findings to clearly define target beneficiary groups, estimate the scale of needs, and prioritise research investment in line with likely impact on policy and citizen outcomes. Analyses the external environment for emerging trends (for e.g., climate change, technological disruptions, socio-economic shifts, etc) and policy developments (for e.g., new regulations, budgetary announcements, international commitments, etc) to assess their implications for program relevance, feasibility, and strategic benefits to citizens and the nation. Develops program proposals based on evidence from research and need analysis, clearly identifying potential risks (for e.g., implementation bottlenecks, changing market conditions, etc.) and opportunities (for e.g., leveraging new technology, synergies with other schemes, etc.) to ensure robust, feasible, and citizen-focused program design.
Levels.Output_Description: I synthesise multi-source evidence to define priority needs and beneficiary segments. I assess data quality, apply structured analysis, and define priority beneficiaries and program outcomes. I develop evidence-based program proposals that address citizen needs, minimise risks, and improve service delivery outcomes.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Leads comprehensive need assessments for major programs by integrating economic, social, technological, fiscal, and operational analyses to inform decision making, and ensuring alignment with national priorities (for e.g., Viksit Bharat@2047, Aatmanirbhar Bharat, Make in India, etc). Critically reviews large-scale datasets, evaluation findings, and expenditure patterns (for e.g., PFMS, OOMF dashboards, sectoral MIS, etc) to identify structural gaps and high impact intervention areas. Evaluates projected public value, cost-effectiveness, and long-term citizen impact of proposed programs before approval or scale-up. Validates assumptions, risk assessments, and beneficiary targeting logic in program proposals to ensure robustness and practicality. Ensures that program proposals clearly articulate tangible and intangible citizen benefits, distributional impact, and measurable outcomes for effective citizen service delivery. Reviews compliance with data governance norms and ethical standards while using advanced analytics tools for decision support. Engages with inter-ministerial, subject-matter experts, senior decision-makers, international organisations and governments to foster new research agendas, validate findings, and incorporate multi-dimensional perspectives into program design. Presents research findings in inter-ministerial meetings, conferences, or policy forums and contributes policy papers or briefs to strengthen evidence-based program decisions.
Levels.Output_Description: I lead complex research assessments to identify high-impact program priorities and citizen benefit areas. I critically evaluate program proposals to ensure feasibility, cost-effectiveness, and measurable public value. I collaborate with ministries, experts, and partners to validate evidence and strengthen program design.

Label: Project KPIs Monitoring
Description: Selects and implements the effective project monitoring tools suiting contract /project requirements. Establishes Key Performance Indicators (like Safety KPIs, Schedule KPIs, Cost KPIs, Procurement/ Contract KPI, Quality KPIs) and sets up reporting, monitoring, assessment mechanisms, dashboards, etc. until project completion, final validation, litigation, and financial close-outs. Ensures continuous monitoring of alignment with the baseline requirement of project design/ scope with respect to execution, and identifies the deviation. Applies technology for capturing real time information/data, performing data analytics.
Area Description: Demonstrates ability to plan phases/stages and identify key activities, dependencies, and milestones for driving projects. Utilises & creates various standard frameworks/mechanisms to determine project mode, vendor selection & engagement, implementation, communication, knowledge transition, and risk mitigation. Evaluates progress against KPIs and ensures adherence to relevant guidelines & policies etc. for ensuring optimization of resource utilization, and driving realization of intended benefit for the intended audience.

Label: Project Evaluation
Description: Evaluate project progress with respect to contractual obligations and various parameters like, governance, communication management, standardisation, Crisis Management Plan, Project Risk Management, Integrated Risk Management, HSE Bottom Line, DCMA 14-point Schedule Health Check, fund release & utilisation, Sustainability (like, Biodegradable, Carbon footprint, Dematerialisation, Design for the Environment (DfE), Environmentally preferable products (EPP), Life-Cycle Assessment (LCA) etc.), Governance Risks, Statutory Risks, Contractual Risks, Operational Risks, Social Bottom Line etc. Proposes necessary adjustments to implementation timelines, utilising efficient and economical methods to accelerate progress.
Area Description: Demonstrates ability to plan phases/stages and identify key activities, dependencies, and milestones for driving projects. Utilises & creates various standard framework/mechanisms to determine project mode, vendor selection & engagement, implementation, communication, knowledge transition, and risk mitigation. Evaluates progress against KPIs and ensures adherence to relevant guidelines & policies etc., for ensuring optimisation of resource utilisation, and driving realisation of intended benefit for the intended audience.

Label: Public Grievance Handling
Description: Demonstrates the capability to receive, assess, process, track, escalate and resolve grievances raised by citizens or stakeholders, using structured mechanisms in accordance with the Centralised Public Grievance Redress And Monitoring System (CPGRAMS) and institutional frameworks. Ensures that every grievance is handled fairly, transparently and efficiently, thus strengthening accountability, enhancing service delivery and maintaining public trust.
Area Description: Ensures citizens’ welfare and citizens’ satisfaction, by improving the standards of accountable and transparent governance, implementing result-oriented administration through innovative interventions based on design thinking, and enhancing citizen-centric decision-making. Implements Open Government policies and practices, which include collaborating and forming partnerships with informed citizens, leveraging social capital, and promoting participative public service delivery by ensuring freedom and ease of access to information. Establish an efficient framework that ensures timely and transparent delivery of services by various government departments through awareness of various social, political, economic and technological developments that may impact citizens.

Label: Maintenance and Disposal of Materials
Description: Oversees and conducts accurate physical verification of materials, valuation of material, analysis of losses etc., and accordingly take required actions for disposal of assets, as per laid down processes/ guidelines in GFR 2017, Manual of Procurement of Goods, 2017, DFPR etc.
Area Description: Demonstrates ability to maintain accurate records of goods and materials in the Ministry / Department / Organisation. Ensures proper custody of delivered items, conducts physical verification of assets, and identifies surplus and obsolete materials. Determines appropriate categories and modes of disposal for goods, such as auction or e-auction, in compliance with GFR, DFPR 1978, and Manual for Procurement of Goods 2017, to minimise losses due to various reasons.

Label: CPGRAMS Portal Management
Description: Demonstrates the ability to monitor reports & dashboards of grievances received, pending for closure, and resolved cases as per the requirement of Ministries/Departments/Organizations for driving effective resolution for pending grievances.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to address grievances promptly and efficiently while prioritiSing transparency and citizen-centricity. Utilizes the Centralised Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS) to manage grievance redressal, ensuring access of the digital systems to all citizens, tracking their status, and obtaining resolutions in accordance with established guidelines and procedures. Measures the effectiveness and efficiency of the Department/Ministry's administration by analysing data and providing feedback for enhancing processes and procedures.

Label: Creation of M&E Framework
Description: Demonstrates the ability to collect and collate data based on defined M&E framework and conduct analysis to outline the performance of government programs in accordance with defined objectives and outcomes.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to develop monitoring and evaluation framework for tracking implementation of central government policies, schemes, and projects for the Department/ Ministry/ Organisation. Identifies right set of data, information, and relevant parameters to evaluate. Undertakes qualitative and quantitative assessments to determine the progress of a government policy, schemes and project towards pre-specified goals and objectives, and to highlight any alarming gap that may hamper the slated outcomes & outputs. Facilitates in making informed decisions and adjustments to improve policy effectiveness, ensuring policies lead to desired outcomes and benefits. Identify successes, challenges, and lessons learned to enhance future policymaking and implementation processes for the Government of India.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Understands the purpose and key components of key monitoring and evaluation frameworks, such as Theory of Change, Results-based Framework, etc, with associated methodologies for data collection and analysis, interpreting their importance for the effective functioning of government programs. Identifies stages of the M&E framework cycle in relation to the program, from setting objectives, selecting SMART indicators, planning data collection methods, to implementation and insights generation. Demonstrates the ability to track indicators, utilise prescribed tools/technologies, and identify bottlenecks in relevant government programs of the M/D/O.  Assists in designing questionnaires and surveys, selecting representative samples, as per the defined M&E frameworks for a program. Uses prescribed tools and methods (for e.g., questionnaires and surveys, scheme-specific IT-applications, such as POSHAN, CPGRAMS, etc) to collect, clean, and compile data.  Documents and reports progress on identified parameters in relevant formats, tools such as schemes’ MIS (for e.g., Poshan Tracker, PMAY MIS), Village/Block level registers & departmental formats, e-Office, etc, periodically.  Follows data-check procedures to ensure accuracy and consistency, and flags errors or inconsistencies or potential biases, as per the DMEO guidelines for data standards. Summarises and shares key information to supervisors by interpreting compiled data, performing basic analysis, and drafting reports. Demonstrates sensitivity to local context and cultural differences while collecting and collating data under the defined M&E framework, ensuring ethical, inclusive, and citizen-centric approaches that strengthen the validity and reliability of programs, against stated objectives and outcomes.
Levels.Output_Description: I understand the basics of monitoring and evaluation frameworks and their importance in government programs.   I collect, clean, and track data periodically using prescribed tools and methods.   I prepare clear summaries of key findings for supervisors based on compiled data.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Develops structured data collection plans (for e.g., defining frequency, sampling strategy, tools, and verification protocols, etc) and applies survey design principles to create and operationalise M&E tools aligned with M/D/O guidelines and scheme objectives. Integrates tools related to data collection, analytics, and visualisation, (for e.g., KoboToolbox, Power BI, Microsoft Excel, GIS, etc) to assist with survey design, conduct preliminary analysis, and present findings in a clear and concise manner. Defines indicators, baselines, targets, and means of verification, and maps risks and assumptions relevant to program objectives to ensure alignment, optimise resources, enable smooth implementation and informed decision making. Supervises the administration of quantitative and qualitative methods (for e.g., surveys, FGDs, KIIs, observational tools, etc) and ensures their ethical and standardised implementation. Conducts data validation and triangulation using field reports, administrative data (for e.g., PFMS utilisation, scheme MIS, etc), and stakeholder inputs to enhance reliability. Synthesises insights using appropriate analysis methods (for e.g., descriptive statistics, cross-tabulation, thematic coding, etc) using tools like SPSS, Stata, R, NVivo. Prepares tailored insight briefs and visual dashboards for different stakeholder groups to inform mid-course corrections.  Manages M&E plans by coordinating with block/district-level teams, implementing agencies, and program units to ensure timely data flow. Analyses the competency gaps among the personnel on aspects of M&E in the team, and administers interventions such as training, mentoring, exposure visits, workshops, etc, to ensure capacity building in the M/D/O.
Levels.Output_Description: I apply M&E frameworks and analysis methods to identify program gaps and recommend improvements.  I interpret and present insights clearly, enabling informed program decisions within the team.  I mentor and support officials to bridge their competency gaps in areas related to M&E.

Levels.Behavioral_Indicators: Sets the overall M&E strategy, aligning program logframes, indicators, and evaluation priorities with national guidelines and the M/D/O’s objectives. Oversees the development of results chains, Theory of Change models, indicator reference sheets, and data quality protocols to ensure clarity, consistency, and accountability across schemes in the M&E framework designs. Reviews program performance against M&E plans, translating evidence into actionable recommendations for decision makers to drive design improvement, policy adjustments, and resource allocation. Strengthens the M&E agenda of the M/D/O by defining data quality assurance frameworks and ensuring their follow-up across schemes’ MIS (for e.g., Poshan Tracker, PMAY MIS, etc), Village/Block-level registers & departmental formats, e-Office, etc. Supervises compliance with protocols and guidelines concerning data collection procedures, data confidentiality, data management, program tracking, stakeholder interactions, ethical usage of AI-integrated tools, etc.  Ensures adherence to evaluation principles of relevance, efficiency, effectiveness, impact, and sustainability, and anchors all M&E approaches in citizen and service delivery outcomes.   Promotes adaptive monitoring and evaluation by combining systems diagnosis, mixed-methods data approach, theory-based assessments and iterative testing to monitor the beneficiary experience, generate insights and guide program improvements with stakeholders. Builds  institutional capacity in M&E by identifying  the capacity gaps  in personnel, technology and processes to build a cohesive environment that bridges the current and future gaps, enabling robust M&E cycles across programmes and schemes. Fosters collaborations and partnerships with line departments, field units, evaluators, research institutions, development partners, and other relevant stakeholders to refine approaches, deepen insights and broaden evidence use, ensuring informed program design and policy implementation.
Levels.Output_Description: I shape and oversee the M/D/O’s M&E strategy to ensure programs generate measurable, citizen-focused impact. I build M&E capacity by enhancing skills, systems, and technology with a forward-looking approach.  I collaborate with stakeholders to translate insights into decisions that enhance the design and effectiveness of government programs.

Label: Dissemination of Information
Description: Disseminates strategic communications, crisis communication, information and awareness about government policies and schemes to the citizens of the country, making pertinent information publicly, freely, and directly accessible, in understandable and comprehensible form and format.
Area Description: Disseminates information through various media channels on behalf of the government to increase public awareness and advocacy of government policies and schemes and addresses any misconceptions using a range of media vehicles such as government websites. Facilitates in generating positive media coverage, building relationships with key stakeholders, managing conflicts or criticisms, and improving public understanding of a Ministry/Department/Organization and its objectives. Utilizes social media to communicate official government policies and information to the public, with a focus on creating an informed citizenry through a people-centric communication approach.

Label: PFMS Portal Management
Description: Utilises the Public Financial Management System (PFMS) to ensure transparent and accountable financial management within the Ministry/Department/Organisation. This includes enrolling and managing implementing agencies and recipients, mapping schemes, and maintaining accurate beneficiary records. Generates, interprets, and analyses PFMS reports, including operational, performance, fund flow, and daily transaction summaries, to track utilisation and identify discrepancies. Performs accounting functions, reconciles PFMS records with physical accounts, and maintains a complete audit trail for compliance with Ministry of Finance rules. Monitors expenses, programs, and grants to ensure funds are released, utilised, and reported in line with approved allocations and financial guidelines. Strength in this competency supports accurate forecasting, informed decision-making, and enhanced efficiency in public financial management.
Area Description: Formulates budget and expenditure plans aligned to the objectives and budget allocation of the Ministry / Department / Organisation, in accordance with the rules and guidelines of the Ministry of Finance, while also forecasting future expenditures. Manages and monitors the Division/ Department's budget, accounts & expenditure, while promoting financial prudence within the Department to enhance its overall efficiencies. Develops proficiency in handling PFMS (Public Financial Management System).

Label: Feasibility and Risk Assessment
Description: Prepares and analyses feasibility study reports (FR), Detailed Project Reports (DPR), Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) & Environmental Management Plan (EMP) Reports to identify potential risks (Governance Risks, Statutory Risks, Contractual Risks, Operational Risks, Financial Risks, Socio-economic Risks, Geopolitical Risks) and proposes suitable solutions for their mitigation.
Area Description: Demonstrates knowledge and ability to identify, define, plan, develop, deliver & monitor government programs, schemes to realize the agreed benefits for the citizens within the prescribed timeframe. Oversees and coordinates a group of related projects within a government program to achieve specific benefits and maintain control. Demonstrates the ability in Program Selection, Program Planning, Program/ Project Transitions, Program Implementation, Strategic Program Management, Program Progress Monitoring and Review, Program Analysis, Program Financing, Program/Project Change Management, Benefits Management, Stakeholder Relationships, and Collaboration.

Label: Court Case Management
Description: Oversees execution of legal procedures in accordance with the National Litigation Policy, as well as follows instructions and stay updated on legal matters from various competent authorities such as CAT, AMRD, AMRCD, and PIMS. Demonstrates understanding of pre-litigation procedures and is adept at effectively managing court cases, notices, stay orders, appellate court directions, and pending appeals, as well as ensuring diligent compliance with conditional implementation.
Area Description: Provides support during legal proceedings pertaining to the Union of India by demonstrating legal know-how and engaging law officers or approved panel counsels for a range of court cases. Keeps track of and manages information and updates for multiple cases on the LIMBS portal to ensure timely, necessary actions.

Label: Change Impact Assessment
Area Description: Adopts a structured approach for the effective initiation and implementation of multi-level, multi-faceted changes in the government ecosystem, with the aim of achieving a prescribed mandate as part of a Govt. program, scheme, project, or government initiative. Drives preparedness to unlearn and relearn, communicates the need for change to gain stakeholder acceptance and shared commitment, and drives change from the current state to the desired future state.

Label: Lateral Thinking
Description: Demonstrates awareness of key emerging trends on a national and global scale. Utilises cross-functional knowledge to develop innovative strategies and approaches to solving real-world problems for the Ministry / Department. Enabling problem solving through consideration of broader perspectives and looking beyond standard reasoning & conventional ideas.
Area Description: Champions ‘out-of-the-box’ thinking, and supports experimentation of new ideas, utilizing technological advancements with the aim of transforming operations to enhance governance efficiency. Synthesizes insights and concepts from diverse disciplines to drive the decision-making process, promote wider acceptance, and encourage cooperation to ensure successful implementation.

Label: Citizen Partnering and Collaboration
Description: Demonstrates proficiency in mediating and engaging with various segments and groups of society to establish a common ground on issues and actions that benefit the entire community. Display “Good Governance” by aiming to enhance effectiveness, efficiency, and accountability through a consensus-based approach that emphasizes equity, inclusivity, and active participation, resulting in increased public satisfaction at lower costs.
Area Description: Ensures citizens’ welfare and citizens’ satisfaction, by improving the standards of accountable and transparent governance, implementing result-oriented administration through innovative interventions based on design thinking, and enhancing citizen-centric decision-making. Implements Open Government policies and practices, which include collaborating and forming partnerships with informed citizens, leveraging social capital, and promoting participative public service delivery by ensuring freedom and ease of access to information. Establish an efficient framework that ensures timely and transparent delivery of services by various government departments through awareness of various social, political, economic and technological developments that may impact citizens.

Label: Provisions on Suspension
Description: Displays understanding of extent, applicability and intent of the CCS (CCA)(Classification, control & Appeal) Rules, 1965- Central Civil Services, Group 'A', Group ‘B’, ‘C’, ’D’ (classification of service, posts), AIS (Discipline & Appeal) Rules, 1969 for officers from All India Services, Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal (CCA)) Rules, 1965 (rule 10); Fundamental Rules (FR) 53 & 54 (b), in relation to provisions and circumstances under which a Government servant may be placed under suspension in the Ministry/Department. Ensures timely payment of subsistence allowance in cases of suspension.
Area Description: Demonstrates a thorough understanding and practical application of administrative vigilance and disciplinary procedures, which serve as safeguards to ensure the proper implementation of government processes, policies, and rules, while identifying instances of breach by government employees who may abuse their authority or power of approval for personal gain. Facilitates building a positive perception and image of the government and its policies, both within the government ecosystem, among citizens, and globally. Adheres to guidelines on implementation of strict anti-corruption measures, vigilant oversight, and a commitment to integrity in public service.

Label: Handling Social Media
Description: Monitors and manages information and narratives on social media platforms, creates and publishes content, engages with and responds to the community of followers/customers, and conducts impact analysis and reporting.
Area Description: Disseminates information through various media channels on behalf of the government to increase public awareness and advocacy of government policies and schemes and addresses any misconceptions using a range of media vehicles such as government websites. Facilitates in generating positive media coverage, building relationships with key stakeholders, managing conflicts or criticisms, and improving public understanding of a Ministry/Department/Organisation and its objectives. Utilises social media to communicate official government policies and information to the public, with a focus on creating an informed citizenry through a people-centric communication approach.

Label: Preventive Vigilance
Description: Fosters an environment of transparency, integrity, and accountability and identify acts of unethical behavioural, fraud and corruption in the ecosystem. Adopts suitable checks and balances to improve systems / procedures to eliminate corruption / discretion in the processes and procedures. Assists in systemic improvements and setting up of effective systems for curbing any potential avenues for corruption in the Ministry / Department / Organisation.
Area Description: Demonstrates a thorough understanding and practical application of administrative vigilance and disciplinary procedures, which serve as safeguards to ensure the proper implementation of government processes, policies, and rules, while identifying instances of breach by government employees who may abuse their authority or power of approval for personal gain. Facilitates in building a positive perception and image of the government and its policies, both within the government ecosystem, among citizens, and globally. Adheres to guidelines on implementation of strict anti-corruption measures, vigilant oversight, and a commitment to integrity in public service.

Label: Government Accounts
Description: Prepares and presents accounts as per Government Accounting Rules, 1990; Accounting Rules for Treasuries; and Account Code Volume III, and classifies transactions in Government Accounts. Ensures that budget heads adhere to the classification rules and other guidelines of GFR 2017 and its relevant amendments.
Area Description: Formulates budget and expenditure plans aligned to the objectives and budget allocation of the Ministry / Department / Organisation, in accordance with the rules and guidelines of the Ministry of Finance, while also forecasting future expenditures. Manages and monitors the Division/ Department's budget, accounts & expenditure, while promoting financial prudence within the Department to enhance its overall efficiencies. Develops proficiency in handling PFMS (Public Financial Management System).

Label: Vendor/Consultant Management
Description: Demonstrates the ability to identify, evaluate, and manage vendors and consultants using established government systems (such as the Government e-Marketplace, GFR 2017, DFPR 1978, and the Manuals for Procurement of Works and Consultancy 2022). Ensures that procurement decisions reflect transparency, competition, economy, efficiency, and accountability, and that agreements are properly executed before commencement of services.
Area Description: Demonstrates the ability to procure goods, services, or works using GeM (Government eMarketplace, National Public Procurement Portal) and other electronic portals, directly or through empanelled agencies, whether by way of purchase, lease, licence, or another method based on fundamental principles of transparency, fairness, competition, economy, efficiency, and accountability with respect to procurement. Ensures judicious use of public funds or any other source of funds (such as grants, loans, donations, or private investment), in accordance with GFR 2017, DFPR, 1978 (DFPR Rule 21, 21(a), 21(b), Manual of Procurement 2022 and their amendments, AOB, TOB, ToR.

Label: Policy Monitoring & Impact Assessment
Description: Creates and implements an effective and practical monitoring & evaluation framework for assessing the impact of policy implementation for maximising the impact and benefits for target stakeholders.
Area Description: Demonstrates the capacity to detect gaps and improvement needs in existing policies in order to translate policy concepts into policy formulation for addressing existing and emerging needs of the citizens. Conducts thorough study and analysis of best practises at State, National, or Global levels, takes use of modern innovation and the most recent advancements in the social, economic, technological, and legal spheres while creating & implementing policies. Focuses on monitoring the application of policy and evaluating its impact and efficacy using a variety of instruments for responsible, open, and sustainable governance.

Label: Implementation and Outreach
Description: Emphasises the use of appropriate methods for executing government programs to ensure the achievement of intended outcomes and impact on the target audience.
Area Description: Demonstrates knowledge and ability to identify, define, plan, develop, deliver & monitor government programs, schemes to realise the agreed benefits for the citizens within the prescribed timeframe. Oversees and coordinates a group of related projects within a government program to achieve specific benefits and maintain control. Demonstrates the ability in Program Selection, Program Planning, Program/ Project Transitions, Program Implementation, Strategic Program Management, Program Progress Monitoring and Review, Program Analysis, Program Financing, Program/ Project Change Management, Benefits Management, Stakeholder Relationships and Collaboration.

Label: Project Implementation
Description: Ensures compliance with the ‘Guidelines for Indian Government Websites’ (GIGW) in maintaining official websites of all Central Government Ministries, Departments, Offices, and CPSEs. This includes keeping data and information up to date and providing user-friendly interfaces and transactions that meet the high expectations of citizens and customers regarding security, the privacy of personal data, and the accuracy of information.
Area Description: Disseminates information through various media channels on behalf of the government to increase public awareness and advocacy of government policies and schemes, and addresses any misconceptions using a range of media vehicles such as government websites. Facilitates in generating positive media coverage, building relationships with key stakeholders, managing conflicts or criticisms, and improving public understanding of a Ministry/Department/Organisation and its objectives. Utilises social media to communicate official government policies and information to the public, with a focus on creating an informed citizenry through a people-centric communication approach.

Label: Legal Know-How
Description: Manages various legal tasks such as preparing and analysing paragraph-wise comments on writs, drafting counter replies or petitions, arranging files for consideration, identifying different case and petition types, working with advance notice of grievances, presenting a comprehensive legal position of the government in affidavit form, and briefing government counsel.
Area Description: Provides support during legal proceedings pertaining to the Union of India by demonstrating legal know-how and engaging law officers or approved panel counsels for a range of court cases. Keeps track of and manages information and updates regarding multiple cases on the LIMBS (Legal Information Management and Briefing System: Centralized government legal case platform) portal to ensure timely and necessary actions.

Label: Maintaining Records of Parliamentary Matters
Description: Demonstrates the ability to ensure maintenance of departmental records of parliamentary proceedings for ready reference, including questions, discussions, notices, etc., in accordance with the established guidelines and standards.
Area Description: Ensures the smooth conduct of Parliamentary proceedings through preparation, maintenance, and management of systems and processes in the Division / Department related to handling parliamentary affairs. Anticipates key questions, supports in preparation of responses/briefs and demonstrates agility in supplying information to key stakeholders.

Label: Scheme/Program Design
Description: Demonstrates the ability to design central government schemes and draft contours of programs (constituent projects), preparation of necessary contract documents for procurement of Materials, Equipment, and  Services, indigenous & imported, in line with the policies and schemes for development of the intended stakeholders.
Area Description: Demonstrates knowledge and ability to identify, define, plan, develop, deliver & monitor government programs, schemes to realise the agreed benefits for the citizens within the prescribed timeframe. Oversees and coordinates a group of related projects within a government program to achieve specific benefits and maintain control. Demonstrates the ability in Program Selection, Program Planning, Program/ Project Transitions, Program Implementation, Strategic Program Management, Program Progress Monitoring and Review, Program Analysis, Program Financing, Program/ Project Change Management, Benefits Management, Stakeholder Relationships, and Collaboration.

Label: Policy Design/Amendment
Description: Demonstrates the ability to draft or amend government policies for delivering citizen-centric civil service basis feasibility analysis and risk assessment of various policy alternatives.
Area Description: Demonstrates the capacity to detect gaps and improvement needs in existing policies in order to translate policy concepts into policy formulation for addressing existing and emerging needs of the citizens. Conducts thorough study and analysis of best practices at State, National, or Global levels, takes advantage of modern innovation and the most recent advancements in the social, economic, technological, and legal spheres while creating & implementing policies. Focuses on monitoring the application of policy and evaluating its impact and efficacy using a variety of instruments for responsible, open, and sustainable governance.
